news_summary: Reuters reports Freeport’s Grasberg copper mine in Indonesia has resumed normal operations after floods damaged smelters and the access road, forcing suspensions in February. Earlier disruptions included a washed-away conveyor belt affecting ore shipments and a lowered Q1 copper sales forecast, suggesting renewed supply support for copper. | American Eagle Gold reported assays from additional 2022 drill holes at its NAK copper-gold porphyry project, highlighting long copper-equivalent intercepts and multiple copper-rich bornite/chalcopyrite zones from near surface to depth. The update supports exploration continuity but does not indicate immediate production, limiting short-term copper price impact. | TNR reports an update on McEwen Mining’s Los Azules copper project in Argentina, where Stellantis closed an ARS $30 billion investment into McEwen Copper. Proceeds are intended to advance the copper project’s development, with Stellantis holding rights to purchase copper output tied to its equity. | Copper Fox announced its 2023 program for the Schaft Creek copper-gold-molybdenum-silver project, including a C$17.2m work plan with geotechnical/metallurgical drilling, environmental baseline sampling, and permitting updates. It focuses on improving mine design and reducing costs, but no near-term production changes are stated. | Sky Gold Corp. says the TSX Venture Exchange accepted its 100% acquisition of three nickel-copper-cobalt-platinum group element properties in Ontario. The deal terms include share issuance, cash, and a net smelter/royalty. No production or development timeline is provided, implying limited short-term supply impact for nickel and copper. | Sky Gold Corp. reports that TSX-V has accepted filing for Sky’s 100% acquisition of three nickel-copper-cobalt-PGE properties in northwestern Ontario. The deal terms involve share issuance and cash payments, plus potential additional consideration if a large, viable ore body is identified—no near-term production impact is stated.

Comex copper futures for March delivery settled down 8.40 cents, or 2.02%, at $4.0840, breaking a three-session winning streak. The move is described as the largest one-day dollar and percentage drop since Feb. 24, with LME copper cash also weaker. | London copper was set for a weekly gain as stronger-than-expected China manufacturing and services data boosted demand sentiment, while declining SHFE inventories suggested improving tightness. Markets also weighed a firmer dollar and potential additional U.S. sanctions on China. Aluminium rose slightly; nickel fell on the day. | SMM data shows Chinese bonded copper inventories rose 11,700 mt to 185,200 mt by March 3, with growth mainly in the Shanghai bonded zone. The rise reflects domestic smelter exports and arrivals under bill of lading. Import losses remain 600–1,200 yuan/mt, prompting traders to warehouse more, and inventories may grow further next week.

SMM reports copper prices rebounded on better global macro data, including stronger China manufacturing PMI and improving eurozone indicators, while US inflation signals triggered dollar volatility. Copper benefited from recovering consumption, falling Chinese inventories, and tighter spot supply as smelters increased exports with limited arrivals. Spot quotes are expected to firm. | The piece reports that copper (along with iron ore) declines as Beijing fails to provide spending pledges. The implied message is softer near-term Chinese demand sentiment, which can reduce expectations for copper consumption and weigh on prices in the coming days. | McEwen Copper reported drill results from the Los Azules porphyry project in Argentina, including 1,052 m averaging 0.29% Cu and a delineation intercept of 236 m at 1.39% Cu (including 42 m at 2.78% Cu). The company says continuity and expansion potential have been de-risked, supporting copper sentiment. | Thiess won a A$155m ($104.8m) letter of agreement to provide mining operations, maintenance, and asset management services for Minera Centinela’s Llano greenfield copper project in Chile. Work is planned to start this month, with emphasis on sustainability and efficient production. Likely limited immediate effect on copper prices. | Electric Royalties announced it will acquire a 0.5% gross revenue royalty (with options for additional royalty) on the Kenbridge nickel project in Ontario. The project is described as nickel-copper mineralized, with a preliminary economic assessment using assumed Ni and Cu prices, but no near-term production changes are stated. | Copper fell 1.5% on Monday after disappointment with China’s ~5% growth target hurt sentiment for industrial metals. Traders also pointed to rising LME inventories and attention ahead of Powell testimony and U.S. non-farm payrolls, which could shift rate and dollar expectations. Aluminium also declined. | Pacific Ridge Exploration acquired an option to earn up to a 75% interest in the Chuchi South porphyry copper-gold project adjacent to its Chuchi property in British Columbia. The company plans drilling and development of copper-gold targets. The release is exploration-focused without any near-term production or supply-impact details. | Queensland’s Premier announced a $5 billion plan to take over and build the CopperString 1100-kilometre electricity transmission project from Townsville to Mount Isa, aiming to expand grid access and connect industries. The move is expected to raise near-term expectations for copper demand from transmission infrastructure.

Queensland will take control of a $5bn, 1,100km high-voltage CopperString transmission line linking Mount Isa to the national grid. The government says it will enable development of “critical minerals” and large renewable generation, with construction expected to begin next year. Costs have risen and state-owned transmission will build it. | China’s customs data show copper imports of 879,000 tonnes in January-February, down 9.3% versus a year earlier. The report links softer global economic activity and weak domestic demand to broad import declines. Analysts note demand may improve into March, but near-term flows remain bearish. | Copper prices edged higher as the dollar weakened and traders anticipated improving China demand from March. LME three-month copper rose slightly, and Shanghai April copper also increased. Aluminium eased on the day and nickel declined, indicating the bullish impulse was strongest for copper rather than industrial metals broadly. | Faraday Copper said it has completed (closed) its Mercer Ranch acquisition tied to the Copper Creek project in Arizona. The update is a company/asset development step that can marginally improve project progress expectations, but it is unlikely to materially change near-term copper supply or demand within days to weeks. | Peru expects unrest to ease, which should restart and release stalled copper shipments. The resumption of export logistics can improve near-term supply availability, easing local tightness. That expectation tends to be supportive for copper, though the magnitude depends on how quickly shipments normalize. | International Copper Association members, including Codelco, Glencore and Freeport-McMoRan, released a roadmap to cut emissions by 30–40% by 2030 and 70–80% by 2040, reaching net zero by 2050. Plans focus on decarbonising power, efficiency and increased scrap use; scope 3 targets are also set with customers. | BC miners increased copper exploration spending by 84% in 2022 to $235 million, according to a BC mineral and coal exploration survey. The report frames copper’s growing importance for clean energy as driving higher exploration budgets. It notes overall exploration spending hitting a decade high, though this is not near-term output. | British Columbia miners boosted copper exploration spending 84% in 2022 to $235 million, with total provincial mineral and coal exploration at a decade high. B.C. produces over half of Canada’s copper and the story links copper demand to renewable power, supporting bullish sentiment for copper. | Highland Copper announced a sequenced development plan for its Copperwood and White Pine North projects in Michigan. Copperwood, fully permitted, will move into early site works in 2023 and target a construction decision in early 2024, while White Pine North progresses with updated PEA, permitting, and infill drilling. Project output is projected over future years. | Ero Copper reported record 2022 copper production of 46,371 tonnes, exceeding guidance, and reiterated 2023 copper production and C1 cost guidance. The company also introduced a 2023 zero-cost collar program hedging ~75% of production with a floor of $3.50/lb and an upside cap, supporting near-term copper revenue visibility.

Comex copper for March delivery settled 2.78% lower at $3.9865 per pound, down 11.40 cents. The move marked the largest one-day dollar and percentage decline since Feb. 24 and left copper down three of the past four sessions, with LME copper cash also weaker. | Peru’s mining production fell 3.61% in January. Copper output reversed from a 19% gain in December to a 1.6% decline. Southern Peru copper assets including Las Bambas, Antapaccay and Constancia saw suspensions or output cuts amid supply and concentrate transport problems, implying tighter near-term copper supply. | Chilean copper output rose in January, with total production reaching 437,900 tonnes, up 2.9% year-on-year. Codelco produced 127,000 tonnes, up 5.1%. The data, released by Cochilco and reported by Mining.com/Reuters, suggests higher near-term supply and potential downward pressure on copper prices. | Copper Road Resources reported exploration updates from its Ontario Copper Road Project, including 3,000 metres of step-out drilling confirming near-surface copper at the Breton Breccia and drilling plans for 2023 on additional Cu-Mo-Au-Ag targets. It affects sentiment on future copper supply but not immediate market balance. | Harita Nickel begins a $600m investor roadshow ahead of a public listing, in a broader Indonesian push to retain nickel processing after the 2019 raw nickel export ban. The country’s EV-battery investments and growing nickel export value support nickel demand/supply expectations, with some peripheral copper IPO plans mentioned. | Panama and First Quantum Minerals agreed to reopen the Cobre Panama copper mine after a three-month shutdown tied to a tax dispute. The new 20-year concession contract raises Panama state “minimum income” and must still be ratified. The restart should ease supply concerns given the mine’s large output in the region. | Panama and a Canadian group have agreed to reopen a major copper mine, signaling restored production after a disruption risk. Such mine restarts can shift near-term supply expectations and influence copper prices over the coming days and weeks as markets reassess availability and inventory outlook. | Aurubis is constructing the Bleed Treatment Olen Beerse (BOB) hydrometallurgical facility to recover copper and nickel from electrolytes/“bleed” streams at its recycling sites, boosting recycling capacity. Production is scheduled for late 2024 with full operations in 2025/26, so near-term price impact is limited despite involvement of both metals. | Aurubis is building the Bleed treatment Olen Beerse (BOB) hydrometallurgical plant to process copper electrolysis bleed and recover metals including copper and nickel. The facility targets higher recovery from electrolyte streams, with commissioning planned for 2H 2024 and full operation in 2025/26, supporting supply expectations. | First Quantum finalized a draft contract to restart Panama’s Cobre Panama copper mine after the government’s maritime authority previously blocked permits to load copper for export. The company expects processing to resume within days and restore full production, subject to public consultation and approvals, removing an operational overhang.

Panama and First Quantum agreed on an operating contract for the Cobre Panamá copper mine, ending a disruption to global copper ore supply. First Quantum plans to recall about 1,000 furloughed workers and resume ore processing operations within days, after suspending processing on Feb 23. | Huatai Securities says China should enter a recovery channel in 2023, with downstream processing operating rates rising sharply from March to April. The report expects the copper and aluminium supply-demand structure to improve, with refined aluminium production restrictions being a key 2023 factor. It also projects a copper shortage turning to surplus in 2024-25. | The article headline says China has seen a burst of copper exports, suggesting more copper leaving the domestic market. It also frames this as evidence the economy remains weak, which can pressure copper prices in the near term by increasing supply and potentially weakening demand expectations. | Huatai Securities expects China’s recovery to improve copper and aluminium supply-demand conditions. Downstream processing enterprise operating rates should rise from March to April. Tighter refined aluminium production restrictions are flagged as an important 2023 market driver, while the copper shortage expected in 2024-2025 could turn into surplus over the next two years. | TNR Gold’s update relays McEwen Copper’s drilling results from the Los Azules porphyry project in Argentina, highlighting copper intercepts such as 1,052 m averaging 0.29% Cu and 236 m of 1.39% Cu, supporting continued resource de-risking and potential expansion toward feasibility in 2024. | First Quantum’s Minera Panamá and Panama agreed to settle a profit-sharing dispute for the Cobre Panamá copper mine, with payments covering taxes and royalties. The draft concession contract requires approval and public consultation, and Minera expects permits to resume copper concentrate loading at Punta Rincon port after a long suspension. | Kazakhstan’s Prime Minister discussed plans to build a copper cathode smelter in East Kazakhstan with capacity of about 300,000 tons per year. The project is intended to attract foreign investment and technology, meet tax and power-supply considerations, and boost exports. State agencies were tasked to support investor implementation. | Stellantis’ multimillion-dollar investment in McEwen’s Los Azules copper project is reported in connection with a royalty and project financing update. Such funding can strengthen expectations for copper project development and eventual supply, which may boost market sentiment and expectations for copper over coming weeks. | US copper scrap exports shifted toward China and India while some other destinations fell. Sources describe depressed domestic demand and sluggish end-market activity, particularly in China. Copper scrap discounts moved wider early 2023 alongside rising COMEX levels. A fire at a US facility may temporarily reroute brass scrap away from exports.

BHP expects China’s demand recovery to support overall growth in copper demand this year, but highlights a short-term copper supply glut. It forecasts increased output from Peru, Chile, Central Africa and Mongolia as projects ramp, with copper ore supply up ~12% by end-2024, plus rising scrap supply. | SMM says copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones rose 10,300 mt to 195,500 mt from March 3, with most gains in Shanghai. The increase reflects domestic smelter exports and arrivals of bills of lading, while contango and cautious trader sentiment reduced warrant shipments. More arrivals are expected mid-March, keeping inventories rising slightly. | ArcWest announced an earn-in partnership with Freeport to advance its Todd Creek copper-gold project in British Columbia. Freeport will fund work expenditures and staged payments to earn up to 80% ownership over multiple years. The news is exploration-focused with no near-term production or supply disruption details. | Deep-South Resources said Namibia’s High Court set aside a decision not to renew Haib Copper’s licence (EPL 3140) and ordered the ministry to restart the renewal procedure. The court cited ignored investment and COVID-19 exploration impacts. The ministry may appeal within 21 court days, creating uncertainty. | Canada bans imports of Russian steel and aluminum to further undermine Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, aiming to weaken Moscow’s war capacity. Officials cite small additional trade exposure: prior tariffs already applied and Russian inflows were limited. The report suggests supply-chain pressure but overall “quite muted” effects; copper impact is indirect and unquantified. | Canada announced a ban on importing Russian steel and aluminum as part of sanctions over the Ukraine invasion. Reported additional curtailed trade is expected to be small because Russian shipments had already declined and imports faced prior tariffs, though the move may add some supply-chain pressure. Copper and nickel are not addressed. | ArcWest shares surged after announcing an earn-in deal with Freeport-McMoRan on its copper-gold property in British Columbia’s Golden Triangle. The news highlights renewed major attention to B.C. copper prospects, which can lift short-term sentiment for copper-linked expectations, even if project impact depends on further drilling and investment. | Canada will ban imports of Russian steel and aluminum under its sanctions against Moscow. Foreign Affairs Minister Joly ties the policy to weakening Russia’s ability to attack Ukraine and possible regime change. Industry sources say Russia’s Canadian aluminum/steel volumes are small and the overall commercial impact is likely muted, limiting price effects.

London copper rose to a nearly one-week high, up 0.2% on the LME, driven by a weaker U.S. dollar and improving China demand signals, including higher spot activity and shipments. The dollar move followed investor expectations after SVB fallout. LME aluminium also gained 0.8% in the same session. | Mongolia’s Oyu Tolgoi copper-gold project began underground production, with Rio Tinto calling it a step toward becoming a major copper producer. The mine is expected to average about 500,000 tonnes of copper per year at peak from 2028-2036, but the near-term market impact is unclear. | Mongolia’s Oyu Tolgoi copper-gold project began underground production. Rio Tinto says the project is a step toward becoming a major copper producer, with total capacity expected around 500,000 tons of copper per year on average from 2028–2036. Start is positive but likely not enough to move near-term copper prices. | Rio Tinto says it has started increasing copper concentrate production from underground ore at the Oyu Tolgoi mine in Mongolia after resolving disputes with the government. The expansion targets eventual higher copper output, supporting expectations for greater copper supply tied to low-carbon demand. Aluminium is only referenced generally. | Rio Tinto says it has started digging and producing underground ore at the Oyu Tolgoi copper mine in Mongolia, after years of delays and disputes. The expansion targets up to ~500,000 tons of copper annually at peak, reinforcing confidence in future copper supply despite a difficult ramp-up. | The article argues copper faces bearish headwinds as demand weakens in China and the U.S. (modest stimulus and softer construction), while supply risks rise. Export disruptions in the DRC have led to copper stockpiling and likely future releases. Codelco plans output ramp, Glencore reopens a mine, and higher rates/macro concerns add pressure.

Comex copper front-month settled 0.59% higher at $4.0695 per pound, gaining 2.40 cents. It rose on three of the past four sessions and notched the highest settlement since March 6. The piece includes year-to-date and month-to-date performance versus past highs and lows, without new drivers. | SMM reports improved copper concentrate availability after the Matarani port blockade in Peru was lifted and production resumed at Indonesia’s Grasberg mine. It also cites an agreement between First Quantum and Panama on Cobre Panama shipments after a prior tax dispute suspension. These developments ease near-term concentrate supply constraints. | SMM reports copper rod plants using copper scrap operated at 54.78% in early March, down 3.74 points week-on-week, due to extremely limited new orders from the civilian cable market and high finished-product inventories. Plants lowered inventories and production. Scrap rod prices are discounted to SHFE, narrowing spreads and pressuring trader purchases. | Panama and First Quantum agreed a new 20-year operating contract for the Cobre Panamá copper mine, guaranteeing minimum annual revenue of $375 million and providing economic terms described as stable and lasting. First Quantum paid $61 million in royalties in 2021, when mine sales were $3.2 billion, indicating improved contract economics and reduced operational risk. | Fathom Nickel increased its land position under the Gochager Lake option agreement by 937 hectares at Mal Lake after the optionor acquired the new disposition. The company also updated Albert Lake exploration status and expects assays later in April-May, but provides no production or reserve changes. | Faraday Copper reported drill intercepts at its Copper Creek project in Arizona, including 100.12 metres at 0.69% copper and other copper-bearing zones outside the current mineral resource shell. Management says results support potential open-pit resource expansion. The news is exploration-focused and does not imply immediate production changes. | Rio Tinto says it has started digging copper from the Oyu Tolgoi mine in Mongolia after years of delays and disputes with the government. CEO Jakob Stausholm argues the project is well-positioned to meet rising copper demand tied to electric vehicles and renewable energy, supporting near-term copper supply expectations. | Comex copper for March delivery settled 1.45% lower at $4.0105, down 5.90 cents per pound. The move marks the largest one-day dollar and percentage decline since March 7, with copper down two of the past three sessions and at its lowest settlement since then.

Quellaveco’s production was insufficient to compensate for a reported drop in Peru’s copper output in January. The shortfall points to weaker near-term supply conditions from one of the world’s key copper-producing countries, which can increase expectations of tighter inventories and weigh on copper prices in the coming weeks. | London copper fell as investors reassessed China demand prospects after early 2020s factory data grew more slowly than expected and property investment stayed weak. A steadier U.S. dollar also dampened buying interest. In parallel, LME aluminium and nickel were slightly lower on the same day. | Tudor Gold updated the Mineral Resource Estimate for its Goldstorm porphyry deposit at the Treaty Creek project, increasing grades and total copper contained to over 3 billion pounds, alongside gold and silver improvements. The release is resource-focused and does not announce production or immediate supply changes. | The article headline says Iran plans to target one million tons of copper production with a USD 10B initiative. This is directly tied to prospective copper supply and could influence market expectations, but the provided text lacks near-term output timing or concrete production changes.

Taseko says it completed its acquisition of an additional 12.5% interest in the Gibraltar copper mine, raising its effective ownership to 87.5%. The deal includes C$60 million minimum payments and contingent amounts linked to Gibraltar copper revenues and copper prices over the next five years. | SMM reports China’s copper concentrate imports by origin in Dec 2022, including 740,600 mt from Chile (+6.34% MoM) and 573,100 mt from Peru (-1.81% MoM). Other flows (Canada, US, Mongolia, Kazakhstan, Indonesia) are also detailed, indicating shifting near-term concentrate supply for Chinese smelters. | SMM reports that blister copper RCs in South China rose 250 yuan/mt in February to 900–1,300 yuan/mt, the highest since mid-2022, driven by sufficient scrap supply and high blister inventories. Imported RCs fell slightly. Smelters restocked before March maintenance, reducing near-term blister demand. | After the Lantern Festival, Chinese copper scrap supply resumed as suppliers shipped more scrap amid high copper prices. While weekly operating rates rose, secondary copper rod plants’ incremental scrap demand stayed limited because wire and cable orders were weak and they relied on existing inventories. Net impact on price appears offsetting. | China’s copper anode imports fell in December 2022 to 84,400 mt (down 7.62% MoM, 6.86% YoY). The MoM drop was mainly due to lower Zambian anode shipments (down 24.49% to 30,800 mt). Imports from Congo rose, while South Africa volumes increased in January. | Ivanhoe Electric reports visible soluble copper mineralization at its Texaco Ridge step-out drill hole, intersecting oxide/chalcocite/chalcopyrite zones, and highlights recent infill drilling at the Santa Cruz deposit (e.g., 55m at 3.10% Cu). The company advances an initial assessment for underground development. | TMC entered a non-binding MOU with Japan’s PAMCO to evaluate toll processing 1.3 million tonnes/year of polymetallic nodules from 2025. PAMCO would use existing Hachinohe smelting infrastructure to produce nickel-copper-cobalt alloy and manganese silicate, with further study on upgrading to matte. Near-term effects are uncertain due to study-only terms. | SMM reported that copper-zinc alloy billet producers’ average operating rate rose to 60.35% in February, up 29 percentage points month-on-month and 17.13 percentage points year-on-year. The higher utilization indicates improved production activity and likely stronger short-term demand for copper alloy feedstock. | TNR Gold reports progress on McEwen Mining’s Los Azules copper project in Argentina, where TNR holds a 0.4% NSR royalty. The update highlights continued exploration drilling, road improvements, and further funding/strategic stakes by Nuton (Rio Tinto venture) and Stellantis, aimed at advancing the project toward a future feasibility update. | TNR Gold reported that Rio Tinto Venture’s Nuton and Stellantis became shareholders of McEwen Copper, funding additional stake increases to advance the Los Azules copper project in Argentina. The release highlights ongoing drilling, updated studies/PEA timing, and a planned heap-leach/ SX-EW approach to produce LME Grade A copper cathodes.

SMM said its monthly Copper Concentrate Index fell to $78.28/mt in February, down $4.94/mt month-on-month, with the decline linked to disrupted mine production and hindered concentrate shipments in America and Southeast Asia. The index was down for 14 straight weeks as of March 10. | SMM said domestic blister copper RCs increased 250 yuan/mt MoM in February, while imported CIF RCs fell $5/mt. Copper was near highs, as higher scrap availability grew after CNY. Improved COVID-19 logistics and faster dismantling eased blister copper supply tightness in China. | SMM estimates China imported 41,000 mt of copper semis in February, +78% MoM, and exported 61,000 mt, +54% MoM. The year-on-year increase is attributed to CNY holiday effects, though Jan-Feb volumes fell YoY. Softer US/EU durable demand is linked to weaker exports and lower freight rates. | SMM says China’s copper semis output rose sharply in February, and operating rates jumped to 64.63%, slightly above expectations. Gains were strongest in the copper pipe industry and supported by improved orders from transformers, new energy, and home appliances post–Chinese New Year, while overseas demand stayed lukewarm. | SMM expects China’s domestic consumption to be in peak season in March, with government support boosting demand for copper semiconductors. This should increase copper semi imports. Overseas demand recovery is expected to be slow, so exports should remain low with no significant rise, supporting domestic market tightness. | SMM says copper prices in March will be heavily influenced by changing expectations for Fed rate hikes, as softer US growth may still support a stronger USD. Import losses have narrowed but continue to weigh on seaborne copper clearance. At the same time, peak season and policy stimulus should lift consumption. | SMM forecasts March operating rates for copper semi-processing in China to average 72.59%, up 7.96 percentage points month-on-month, driven by the traditional peak season. Sector-specific rates are expected at 83.55% for copper pipe, 76.61% for plate/sheet and strip, and 69.24% for cathode rod. | Mistango (15% interest) updates Manibridge drilling results, highlighting over 1% nickel mineralization and a highest-grade continuous intersection of 19.45m at 1.69% NiEq, with assays also incorporating Cu and Co components. These are exploration findings rather than production changes, so near-term metal price effects are likely limited. | The European Union published its Critical Raw Materials Act and designated copper and nickel as critical raw materials. The framework aims to simplify permitting and strengthen access to supply. While potentially supportive for medium-term investment certainty, the excerpt provides no immediate trade or supply shock likely to drive near-term prices. | The article argues copper should benefit from supply tightness, historically low inventories, and ongoing disruptions, reinforced by EU legislation classifying copper and nickel as critical raw materials. It also expects aluminum to remain above historical averages into early 2024. Nickel is framed as having a 2023 surplus despite EV-driven demand.

LME copper closed higher on the week, with SHFE futures also up slightly. The move is attributed to eased worries about the banking crisis after bank stocks fell, alongside a large drawdown in Chinese copper inventories and increased downstream procurement as prices dropped. Overall, prices are expected to trade narrowly. | SMM reports Shanghai copper spot premiums fluctuated, with premiums rising on downstream buying as prices fell, but subsequently dropping. It expects more imported copper inflows due to a favorable domestic SHFE/LME ratio and strong premiums, plus increased warrant sales after delivery, pressuring intraday spot quotes. | SMM expects panic sentiment to fade as overseas liquidity injections reduce systemic risk, while consumer demand and fundamentals stay broadly bullish. Copper market structure remains backwardated, with rebounding premiums and improving import losses. However, rising imported-copper inflows may pressure domestic supply and slow inventory depletion, weighing on spot premiums. | SMM reports China’s copper inventory across major trading markets fell 44,900 mt to 226,200 mt as of March 17. Copper semi-processing companies replenished stock after prices tumbled. The article argues that downstream recovery and lower inventories should underpin copper prices over the near term. | SMM reports that Shandong spot copper discounts had stabilized early in the week but moved up and down with copper price volatility after a banking crisis. Downstream procurement increased during price drops but eased after rebounds. This week demand is improving slightly, yet discounts are expected to fall back toward 100 yuan/mt. | SMM reports active spot copper concentrate transactions and recent improvement in logistics for Las Bambas in Peru after a roadblock removal, with gradual inventory reduction and possible late-March shipments. Port congestion persists, but inventories at Chinese ports fell, while smelters expect TCs to rebound in April and into Q2. | Industrial metals prices fell as investors worried that a banking crisis could worsen and hurt global economic growth. Copper eased on both LME and SHFE. Nickel also declined. Aluminium was mixed but broadly pressured, reflecting risk-off positioning as liquidity promises and a Credit Suisse rescue deal failed to calm markets. | ADVENTUS and Salazar announced additional drilling results at the El Domo underground deposit, reporting 14 metres at 4.44% copper equivalent. The update is a positive exploration/resource signal for the project and can provide modest support to expectations for copper supply, though likely with limited immediate price impact.

Nevada Copper filed audited financials and provided an update on Pumpkin Hollow’s underground mine restart. It says vent shaft stripping, ore-handling excavation, and development toward higher-grade stoping are underway and it expects to ramp to nameplate milling capacity by end-2023, with mill restart in late Q3 2023. | LME copper rose 1.15% to $8,711/mt and SHFE 2305 finished higher. The US dollar index fell after UBS’s low-priced Credit Suisse acquisition eased worries. SMM data show Chinese copper inventories dropped week-on-week with destocking and downstream replenishment, while import inflows increased, keeping prices range-bound but supported. | Reuters reports copper prices in London fell Tuesday as the dollar recovered from a five-week low and traders worried about a looming global financial crisis. While some risk appetite returned after UBS’s Credit Suisse takeover, LME three-month copper eased 0.4%. Aluminium rose slightly, while SHFE nickel fell 2.6%. | London copper fell as the dollar rebounded from a five-week low and traders grew cautious about a potential global financial crisis. After UBS’ takeover of Credit Suisse eased some systemic banking fears, risk sentiment briefly improved, but copper still slipped 0.4% on the LME. | Kodiak Copper Corp. says it is mobilizing for a 2023 exploration program at its MPD copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia. Plans call for up to 25,000 metres of drilling across multiple targets, alongside geophysical and geochemical surveys, to identify new high-grade porphyry centers. | Hannan Metals signed an agreement to acquire an option on the Cerro Rolando copper porphyry project in northern Chile. The company plans initial ground magnetics/EM surveys and potential drilling after permits. The news is primarily exploration-focused and does not indicate immediate supply disruptions or demand shifts. | Pan Global says the EU’s 2023 Critical Minerals and Metals List includes copper, highlighting the potential for EU support such as funding opportunities, tax incentives, and faster permitting. The firm expects this to benefit its Escacena copper project in Spain, aiding advancement and reliable EU copper supply expectations. | Midland Exploration begins a C$3.4m nickel-focused exploration program in Nunavik, including lake sediment and airborne ZTEM surveys targeting a crustal-scale structure favorable for Ni-Cu-Co mineralization. It builds on prior Ni-Cu discoveries and shows in grab and channel samples but provides no immediate production or supply changes. | Universal Copper terminated an earlier private placement and announced a new non-brokered financing of up to 48,000,000 units to fund ongoing operations and exploration for its Poplar copper project in British Columbia. The release is primarily corporate financing, with only indirect near-term implications for copper supply expectations.

Market commentary links copper to possible supply shortfalls without sufficient project investment (UBS) and to Macquarie’s lower mine growth guidance for 2023, suggesting a tighter market this year. It also flags potential surpluses in 2024, which could weigh later, limiting clear immediate direction. | Hannan Metals signed an agreement with an option to acquire 100% of the Cerro Rolando copper porphyry project in Northern Chile. The move is aimed at securing development of a new copper resource in a prolific metallogenic belt, potentially supporting expectations for future supply.

The article title says Kodiak Copper has begun its 2023 exploration program at the MPD project. While this is directly tied to a copper project, the available content provides no evidence of immediate mine output changes, disruptions, or inventory impacts that would likely move copper prices within days to weeks. | The piece cites a Comex Copper market data update: the front-month contract settled $4.1460, up 1.90% (7.75 cents/lb). Copper rose for six straight sessions and posted its largest one-day dollar and percentage gain since late February 2023, signaling bullish momentum. | Comex Copper (March delivery) settled 1.90% higher at $4.1460 per pound, up 7.75 cents. The contract marked its largest one-day dollar and percentage gain since Feb. 28 and extended a six-session winning streak, reaching the highest settlement since March 1. | Wood Mackenzie says China’s renewed growth would most benefit steel, aluminium and copper, with housing/property recovery lifting construction-linked demand. In a high-growth scenario it estimates additional copper and aluminium consumption and suggests that higher energy demand could trigger supply curtailments that can support nickel and other metals. | Wood Mackenzie expects China’s reopening and faster growth to raise construction-driven demand for copper and aluminium, with additional tonnes forecast for both. In a higher-growth scenario, exporters and stronger activity could support metal prices. It also warns higher energy demand may trigger supply curtailments, potentially boosting nickel as well.

Copper and other base metals in London climbed after China cut a key borrowing rate for the first time in 10 months to support growth. The Lobito corridor should lower export logistics costs and transit times for copper concentrate. | Ivanhoe Mines’ Kamoa Copper venture in Congo signed an MoU to ship copper concentrate by rail through the Lobito Corridor to Angola’s Atlantic port, reducing distance versus trucking. An initial up to 10,000 metric tons shipment is planned in Q4, supported by prior U.S. rail-investment plans. | Murchison Minerals begins a 2023 summer exploration program at its HPM nickel-copper-cobalt project in Quebec, combining prospecting with a deep ground EM survey to refine targets for future drilling. The firm cites prior high-grade Ni and Cu intercepts, but no immediate production or supply changes are reported. | MTB Metals says drilling is expected to begin around Aug. 19 on its Telegraph copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia’s Golden Triangle. The program targets the Dok trend with 2,000–3,000 meters planned, supported by soil geochemistry and geophysical surveys showing copper anomalies consistent with porphyry systems. | Murchison Minerals began a 2023 summer exploration program at its Quebec HPM nickel-copper-cobalt project, combining prospecting with a deep ground EM survey to identify sulphide targets at depth. The company cites prior high-grade Ni/Cu intercepts and aims to de-risk future drilling; it is not a near-term supply change. | Unicoin says it will acquire rights to explore and exploit the Barda Gonzalez copper project in Argentina for about $210M, pending due diligence and concession transfer. The deal is primarily promotional/financing-focused and lacks near-term production or supply details, limiting immediate copper price impact.

Egyptian coppersmiths in Cairo’s Al-Jamaliya area say rising global copper prices have cut demand, pushing consumers toward cheaper substitutes (including aluminum) and some craftsmen to abandon copper sheets. The dollar’s strength weighed, but the liquidity boost improved sentiment. Aluminium eased, while nickel rose slightly on both LME and SHFE, reflecting mixed but supportive macro conditions. | Reuters reports that low reported China copper inventories have coincided with a rise in LME net long positions to a six-month high. However, traders and manufacturers say spot copper is plentiful and demand is weakened by a slowing economy and sluggish property/export activity, limiting near-term upside for prices. | Copper snapped back from a 2-1/2 month low as China’s central bank injected liquidity and state banks sold dollars to buy yuan, supporting the renminbi. LME three-month copper rose 0.6%. A firmer dollar capped gains. Aluminium and nickel also advanced on the session’s risk/FX-driven move. | Aston Bay and American West report diamond-drill hole ST23-04 at the Storm Copper Project intersected a thick 18.5m interval of visual chalcopyrite/chalcocite and a copper-bearing fault zone, extending the prospective horizon. Assays are pending and drilling continues, indicating potential for further copper discoveries. | Torq Resources started a ~4,000 m reverse-circulation drill program at its Margarita IOCG project in Chile to test the Falla 13 discovery and additional anomalies. The company plans to spend $2.5 million on exploration over 36 months. Reported drilling highlights high-grade copper intervals, but no production or supply changes are announced. | Southern Copper’s call reviews the copper market, noting the LME copper price down 11% year-on-year and current prices near $3.90/lb amid concerns over China slowdown and weaker Europe/U.S. demand. Management cites expectations of a ~100,000-ton surplus but also highlights a sharp inventory decline, keeping the outlook mixed. | Copper is reported to rise in tandem with iron ore as traders weigh concerns around China’s property sector. The market reaction suggests investors see a near-term balance favoring higher copper prices, likely through updated demand expectations and broader industrial-metals sentiment, rather than immediate downside dominance from property woes.

LME and SHFE copper prices rebounded overnight, with east and south China spot quotes rising on tighter spot cargoes and low inventories. The report notes a weaker-than-expected US unemployment claims figure and China’s commitment to prevent exchange-rate overshooting, which supported the yuan. Still, the article suggests copper has limited upside room near term. | Orexplore Technologies won a $1.55m contract with BHP to digitise and scan drill core at the Carrapateena iron-oxide copper-gold mine in South Australia. Further inventory declines are expected. | Copper is described as drifting lower, with confidence sapped by concerns about China. The article framing implies weaker near-term demand expectations tied to China, which typically pressures copper prices in the following days as traders adjust sentiment and positioning. | Merdeka Copper Gold said it is ramping up production, including Wetar Copper Mine output of copper cathodes and expected higher copper volumes in 2H 2023 with added pyrite-ore deliveries for further processing. Copper rebounded after three weekly declines as China’s central bank pledged to resolve local government debt risks and cut the one-year lending rate, boosting sentiment. Low inventories also supported prices. LME and SHFE showed small gains in aluminium and nickel as well, reflecting broader risk-on demand expectations. | Chorus reported a 61% profit drop, citing higher interest rates and increased depreciation costs after shutting down copper network areas and expanding fibre. It said copper connections fell by about a third to 240,000 remaining, reinforcing a transition away from copper broadband. No explicit aluminium market linkage was provided. | Copper prices rebounded after a third weekly fall, supported by sharp SHFE stock declines and low inventories plus expectations of autumn demand. However, gains are capped by weak Chinese economic/property data and liquidity risks. China cut the one-year lending rate, adding support, while aluminium and nickel rose modestly. | Copper Road Resources announced the start of a 2023 1,000-metre diamond drilling program at its JR Zone copper project in Ontario. The company targets shallow copper-rich porphyry and Richards breccia zones and plans to test previous intercepts. The release is exploratory and not a production or supply disruption update. | Mirasol Resources said its preliminary drilling at the Sobek copper-gold project in Chile was inconclusive and suspended early due to winter weather, with two holes halted by depth and structural factors. The company plans follow-up drilling in Q4 2023 and notes surface anomalies with up to 2,200 ppm copper. | US Copper Corp. reports completion of a 2023 exploration drilling program at its Moonlight-Superior copper project in California, targeting oxide caps and collecting samples for assay results expected in September. It highlights a large copper resource and mentions U.S. copper critical-materials support, but provides no immediate production impact. | Talon Metals’ shares rose as drilling at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota returned high-grade results, including a long intercept with meaningful nickel and copper grades. The upbeat exploration update can support sentiment for nickel and copper by improving expectations for future supply. | Romanian officials say a Turkish company may join to build a copper ore processing plant tied to Cupru Min Abrud’s output. Talon Metals announced drilling results from the Tamarack Nickel Project: 101.71 meters at 3.04% nickel-equivalent, including 1.94% nickel and 1.84% copper. The work, supported by a Columbia ARPA-E grant, also targets novel processing to lower CO2 footprint and increase nickel/copper recovery, improving future supply outlook. | KGHM signed a long-term sales agreement with NKT to supply copper wire rod for 2023–2027, with a total value up to PLN 14.2 billion depending on options. The contract reinforces steady demand and delivery of copper semi-products used in wire and cable for the energy transition. | NKT signed a long-term agreement with KGHM to purchase a minimum 52,500 tons of copper wire rod annually from 2023 to 2027, with options to expand. NKT says copper is critical for power cables for renewables and electrification, supporting its supply security and growth. | The piece argues copper is set for higher prices as the energy transition boosts demand for electrification, transmission, EVs and renewable technologies. It notes supply constraints from permit/financing challenges, ongoing M&A and mine ramp-ups, and miner stock re-ratings tied to stronger copper-price environments. | NKT signed a long-term agreement with KGHM to supply at least 52,500 tons per year of copper wire rod from 2023-2027, with options to expand quantities and extend the contract. KGHM is an integrated producer with mines, smelters, and rod facilities in Europe, helping secure NKT’s copper needs. | NKT signed a long-term supply agreement with KGHM for a minimum of 52,500 tons per year of copper wire rod from 2023–2027, with options to increase volume and extend the deal. The contract secures a substantial share of NKT’s copper needs for power cable production, supporting copper demand sentiment. | Palladium One reports highly anomalous nickel (up to 785 ppm) and copper (up to 431 ppm) soil geochemistry between the West Pickle and RJ Zones at its Tyko Ni-Cu-Co project, supported by ultramafic mapping and geophysics. The news may modestly improve sentiment on future Ni/Cu supply, though no production change is indicated. | Palladium One reports highly anomalous nickel and copper (plus cobalt) soil results at its Tyko project, linking the West Pickle and RJ zones via interpreted chonolith/feeder dykes. It also states it earned an 80% interest in the Pezim II block. The news supports exploration potential but is not production-related. | Copper prices rose for a fourth straight session, supported by firmer Chinese refined copper demand (ICSG) and additional Chinese monetary support, alongside a weaker US dollar. LME three-month copper has oscillated in a tight range since May, with fund positioning swinging between large longs and shorts. | Copper prices rose for a fourth session, up 1.2% on the LME, driven by stronger-than-expected Chinese refined copper consumption data and additional Chinese monetary/support measures. Akipress reports that Kazakhstan and Mongolia are among four countries that supply a large share of copper exports to China, citing an Asian Development Bank report. The piece is descriptive and does not mention any export changes, disruptions, or policy measures that would directly pressure copper prices in the next days. | Challenger Exploration reported surface channel sampling at its El Guayabo copper-gold project in Ecuador suggesting mineralisation extends about 200 metres above the current resource boundary. The findings are exploration-positive for future copper output. | Palladium One announced highly anomalous nickel and copper soil results between the West Pickle and RJ zones at its Tyko project, including values up to 785 ppm Ni and 431 ppm Cu. No production or supply impact is indicated. | ICSG reported the global refined copper market was oversupplied by 213,000 tonnes in the first half of the year. It cited a 90,000-ton supply gap in June and a 58,000-ton shortage in May, alongside June production of 2.25 million tonnes versus consumption of 2.34 million tonnes. | ICSG reported the global refined copper market shifted from a 196,000-ton deficit in the first half of 2022 to a 213,000-ton surplus in Jan–Jun 2023. China and the DRC increased output while demand softened in the EU, Japan and the US. June still showed a smaller deficit. | The report argues Zambia’s government copper-production target (3mt by 2030) lacks credible plans, noting mines lack expansion capacity and new-mine timelines are unrealistic. It highlights production stagnation despite earlier forecasts and raises doubts about additional copper supply for the renewable transition, potentially weighing on copper sentiment. | Copper and most base metals rose in London as improved demand prospects from top consumer China—supported by upbeat Chinese copper consumption data and speculation of further government measures—boosted sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also increased alongside copper. | Moneta Gold announced it completed transactions to expand its Loveland Nickel property near Timmins, adding claims around the Cominco and Hollinger zones. The report keeps a neutral daily trend and notes copper remains in a corrective mode, with key resistance above $4.40 and support at $3.56/$3.19 on Nymex copper futures. It advises staying sidelined until a breakout sticks, suggesting limited near-term directional conviction absent a technical trigger. | American Copper Development Corp. reports preliminary DCIP/MT survey results over its Lordsburg property in New Mexico, identifying porphyry-style signatures and historic high-grade copper zones. The company plans a ~5,000m drill program mid-February to test copper mineralization at depth. It’s a sentiment-driven exploration update, not a supply change. | London copper pulled back 0.7% after a five-session rally, driven by unease over high interest rates and weaker global growth expectations, which outweighed tentative demand optimism from China. Technical resistance also limited upside. LME aluminium and nickel fell alongside copper as investors awaited Jackson Hole. | BlackRock’s Latin America investment trust reports November outperformance versus its Latin America equity benchmark and highlights that Ero Copper helped performance as copper prices rose. The piece discusses regional macro and portfolio changes, but only copper is directly tied to metal price movement, via the cited miner’s benefit. | The announcement is a BlackRock Latin America investment trust monthly performance update. Reuters reports copper fell on Friday as a strong U.S. dollar increased costs for non-dollar buyers. Three-month LME copper dropped and SHFE October copper weakened. It also cites weaker China demand data and shows aluminium and nickel easing on SHFE, indicating broader near-term pressure from FX and macro sentiment. | Reuters reports London/LME copper is set for its biggest weekly gain in four, supported by Chinese policy measures stabilizing the yuan and hopes for stronger consumption in coming months. A firmer dollar caps gains. Aluminium is nearly flat, while nickel edges slightly higher, reflecting limited spillover. | The piece frames copper as the best “bullish” investment tied to the shift from fossil fuels to renewable energy, suggesting rising demand expectations for copper from electrification and renewables. It is a thematic/argumentative view rather than a report of immediate supply disruptions or inventory changes. | Reuters reports Codelco’s new CEO Ruben Alvarado will tackle a sharp copper production decline to its lowest level in 25 years, address rising costs and debt, and manage operational setbacks. Codelco is also tasked with developing Chile’s lithium push. The production focus is supportive for short-term copper sentiment. | European copper cathode premiums fell as weak demand in Germany and Europe, high financing costs, and subdued spot liquidity pushed sellers to offer more material back to traders. US premiums also softened with thin spot activity. In contrast, China premiums rose on improved import arbitrage and tighter spot availability. | Copper is set for its strongest week in a month after LME prices rise on expectations of a China housing-demand uplift, supportive seasonal consumption, and short-covering. Copper prices rose as the dollar weakened ahead of major U.S. data, while China’s stock-market-support measures improved risk sentiment. The update also notes Rio is selling its majority stake. Markets await China PMI data for demand signals. Aluminium also edged higher, while nickel was slightly down, reflecting mixed metal-specific flows. | Sibanye says it remains on the shortlist to buy Zambia’s Mopani Copper Mine assets, in a government process expected to select a bidder by end-July. The company is pursuing a copper portfolio expansion amid weaker recent profits. The article also notes prior nickel asset purchases, but without further near-term specifics. | Zijin Mining plans about $3.8bn to expand Serbia’s Cukaru Peki copper-gold mine, adding infrastructure, power supply, and flotation capacity and extending drilling to near 2km depth. The project targets around 1.2mt copper production by 2025, up sharply from 2017 levels, reinforcing copper supply growth expectations. | Sibanye is still among potential buyers for Zambia’s state-owned Mopani Copper Mines, with Zambia aiming to choose a bidder by late July and moving into due diligence and exclusive negotiations. The company’s broader expansion includes nickel assets, but the near-term market focus is copper supply and ownership expectations. | Rio Tinto sold 55% of its stake in the La Granja copper project in Peru to First Quantum for $105m, with First Quantum also planning to fund up to $546m to take the project through feasibility and toward development. The deal targets future copper supply needs, but near-term price impact is unclear. | Peru cut its growth forecast to 1.1% for 2023 amid a weaker first-half 2023 contraction and warned further revisions are possible. It expects private mining investment to fall 4.5%. The report also notes declining copper price assumptions for 2023-24, pressuring copper sentiment. | Sibanye Stillwater says it will bring a Chinese investor as a partner if it wins the bid for Zambia’s Mopani Copper Mines. The company is on a shortlist competing with Zijin. The partner is meant to reduce technical and investment risk, but the article provides no immediate supply shock.

SMM reports copper prices rising on the day (LME up about 1.15%, SHFE also higher). The U.S. JOLTS data eased labor-market pressure, boosting bets on fewer Fed hikes and a weaker dollar. South China copper inventories fell for nine straight days, tightening spot supply and helping sentiment despite lukewarm downstream buying. | London copper edged down 0.1% to $8,443/t as traders stayed cautious ahead of key China and U.S. economic data that could steer interest-rate expectations and demand outlook. LME aluminium rose 0.3% while nickel fell 0.2%, indicating mixed, low-magnitude pressure across industrial metals. | London copper slipped as traders took a cautious stance ahead of key U.S. and China data. A softer dollar and China mortgage easing in Guangzhou limited declines. Copper premiums widened on higher London–Shanghai spreads. Nickel fell and aluminium edged up, reflecting mixed macro-driven sentiment rather than a specific metal supply shock. | Copper prices slipped on concerns about weaker global growth and metals demand, with investors reassessing softer U.S. jobs data. Losses were moderated by signs of support for China’s property sector. Aluminium rose, while nickel eased, reflecting mixed sentiment ahead of further China manufacturing data. | Taseko announced that a Programmatic Agreement with the US EPA, covering treatment and protection of cultural resources, has been executed for its Florence Copper project and is now in effect. Aluminium rose while nickel declined, reflecting mixed macro-demand signals across industrial metals. | Peru lowered its 2023–2024 growth outlook, citing weak private investment in the copper industry, El Niño disruption, and social unrest. As a major copper producer, the outlook is bearish for near-term copper sentiment. | Taseko said construction at its Florence Copper project is complete and wellfield acidification and SX/EW plant commissioning are progressing, with copper cathode expected within weeks. Codelco cut its 2023 output goal due to delays in deposit improvement projects, with production not expected to approach historical levels until 2030. | Benton Resources received conditional TSX Venture Exchange approval for an option to earn 70% of Spruce’s Newfoundland Great Burnt copper-gold project, pending disclosure of NSR royalties. Sandfire Resources is described as being at an “exciting juncture” while it ramps up global copper assets, including a shift away from Western Australia. Copper is also present in assays. | Copper fell 1.1% after China’s manufacturing PMI contracted for a fifth month, reinforcing demand distress. Grab samples returned assays up to 3.39% copper, with additional copper-molybdenum results. Soil assays are pending, so near-term tradable copper supply impact is limited. | Aurubis said it identified “considerable discrepancies” in its target metal inventory. The company noted related damages could be in the low three-digit-million-euro range and that losses would affect fiscal 2022/23 results. The disclosure adds short-term uncertainty around copper availability and production economics. | Peru’s Minsur said it will invest at least $2.25 billion to expand copper operations, including $1.8 billion at the Justa mine and $450 million for modernization of Minera Raura. Justa copper reserves are expected to rise 30%, and the mine produced 126,036 tonnes of copper last year. | Minsur plans to invest at least $2.25 billion to expand its copper operations in Peru. Such large expansion spending can shift market expectations about future copper availability and production growth, which may support copper prices in the near term. The timing of output ramp-up remains uncertain.

Copper prices rose 0.8% early Sept 1 as China issued further measures to revive its crisis-hit property sector, including easing borrowing rules and lowering mortgage rates. The improved demand outlook supported broader industrial metals, with LME aluminium and nickel also up around 0.8-1.1% despite a strong U.S. dollar. | LME and SHFE copper prices fell modestly as US core PCE rose moderately but inflation-adjusted consumption increased more than expected and the US dollar rebounded. At the same time, East China spot premiums rose on tighter spot supply and South China inventories continued falling, while downstream buying stayed cautious under high prices. | Copper hit a four-week high as China announced measures to support its crisis-hit property market and manufacturing activity expanded. Analysts attributed the rally to improving demand indicators such as new orders in PMIs and expectations of metals restocking as construction resumes. Aluminium and nickel also rose alongside copper. | Europe’s top copper producer Aurubis said a scrap-metal scam involving falsified supplier details and internal sampling cover-ups created serious recycling inventory shortfalls and could cost low three-digit million euros. It now expects weaker operating earnings, potentially tightening near-term copper supply sentiment and adding bearish pressure to copper prices. | Aurubis, Europe’s largest copper cathode producer, said an inventory review found significant discrepancies tied to suspected criminal activity, with damages potentially in the low three-digit million euros. The theft may prevent the firm from meeting its profit forecast. The shares fell about 12%, highlighting supply-inventory concern. | Aurubis said it found inventory discrepancies after scrap suppliers manipulated shipment details and employees helped conceal a copper shortfall. The missing metal was detected over weeks in processing. Losses could reach the low three-digit-million-euro range, forcing it to cut earnings guidance. Shares fell sharply. | FTSE 100 rebounded as basic resources and energy stocks rose. Rio Tinto and Glencore gained on higher copper prices, while BP and Shell benefited from oil rebounding to year-high levels. Other company items were mostly unrelated. The piece offers market-move context rather than a new copper catalyst. | Aurubis says it may face large losses after discovering discrepancies in its metals inventory tied to manipulated invoices for shipments that never reached its site. The affected inputs include copper and precious metals. The firm has downgraded forecasts and is investigating potential criminal activity, raising near-term risk perception in metals markets.

A rise in copper thefts has damaged NSW power poles, rail infrastructure, and telecommunications, with thieves motivated by high copper scrap prices on international markets. Authorities cite weak enforcement and increased thefts, creating local scrap scarcity for compliant operators and utilities, though the article doesn’t show a broad global copper supply disruption. | Mopani Copper Mines in Zambia reported worsening H1 losses to $196 million as production slumped to 14,946 tons from 20,000 tons a year earlier and costs rose. ZCCM is seeking a new investor after taking over from Glencore, with plans to expand output via further funding. | Copper prices fell as markets worried about slower demand from China’s property sector and as LME-registered copper inventories rose sharply. A softer US dollar limited losses, but copper still declined. Aluminium also eased, while nickel dropped amid concerns over Indonesia delaying mining quota issuance for nickel supply. | Metals One said initial diamond drilling at its SRH Rana project in Norway confirmed high-grade nickel-copper-cobalt sulphide mineralisation at the Bruvann prospect. Reported intercepts include 1.0%+ nickel with copper grades around 0.14–0.18%. The mineralisation is open along strike, with further drilling planned by the farm-in partner. | Metals One said early diamond drilling at its SRH Rana project in Norway confirmed high-grade nickel-copper-cobalt sulphide mineralisation, including intercepts with up to 1–2% nickel and notable copper values. The company expects further drilling updates. Near-term fundamentals likely remain unchanged, limiting price impact on nickel and copper. | Phoenix Copper says metallurgical testing at its Empire mine in Idaho indicates robust copper recoveries using flotation and sulphuric acid leaching. Navarre Creek drilling is underway. While results may improve future project economics, they do not imply near-term supply changes, limiting any immediate effect on copper prices. | Copper prices retreated 0.9% to $8,425 as traders worried about China demand, particularly slower property activity, and about rising LME warehouse inventories. Losses were limited by a softer dollar. Aluminium also fell. Nickel edged lower, despite separate supply concerns tied to Indonesia mining quota delays.

Overnight trading saw LME copper dip about 0.49% and SHFE copper fall about 0.44%. SMM data show Chinese inventories rose to 96,700 mt (+8,100 mt on the week), driven by higher imports and arrivals, while downstream demand remains weak due to high prices. Copper gains appear limited. | Copper fell as LME exchange inventories rose and investors worried about demand amid soft economic data from China. The release is copper-focused and provides no near-term nickel or aluminium drivers. | Kutcho Copper Corp. reports target generation for its Kutcho copper-zinc project, highlighting high-priority drill areas such as Esso West, with historical intercepts including 7.2m at 2.0% Cu and plans for ground and drill testing in 2024. Rio Tinto’s WA environmental submission for its proposed Winu copper mine outlines plans to generate most electricity from solar and wind, cutting emissions by 75%. China’s November copper imports rose 10.1% to the highest level in almost two years, helped by dwindling stocks and a stronger yuan that made copper imports cheaper. Robust orders from grid and renewables boosted cable and wire makers’ output, tightening market inventories and supporting spot premiums. | London copper prices were flat as traders waited for catalysts. LME aluminium slipped slightly, while nickel rose sharply; however, overall moves suggest limited new directional impetus. China export growth helped cushion sentiment. | HANetf and Sprott Asset Management launched an ESG-screened Ucits ETF focused on copper miners, tracking the Nasdaq Sprott Copper Miners ESG Screened Index. The rally was tempered by concerns over broader economic growth and pending U.S. payrolls, while aluminium fell slightly. | Midland Exploration and SOQUEM reported new high-grade Cu-Au-Ag showings at their Nachicapau property in Nunavik, including discovery of six new mineralized veins at surface and assays up to 20.4% Cu. Pampa Metals contracted Eco Minera to conduct a ~2,500m diamond drilling program at its Piuquenes porphyry copper-gold project in Argentina, aimed at testing depth and lateral extension of known mineralization. China’s exports showed recovery and copper imports increased 10.1% to the highest in nearly two years, with import premiums near a one-year high. The LME copper and Shanghai contracts climbed. | SMM data show that as of 15:00 CST on Dec 8, the copper cathode spot trading intensity index decreased to 40.52, down 18.1 from the previous day. The article is copper-focused but remains exploration-stage with no production or supply change. | Anglo American said it will cut near-term production and capital spending to lower costs amid weak Chinese demand and macro uncertainty. The directive raises concerns about an imminent reduction in copper output and supply availability. Markets typically respond by re-pricing near-term copper supply risk, contributing to downward pressure on copper prices over the next several sessions. | Panama formally ordered First Quantum to shut down its flagship copper mine. The decision raises expectations for a near-term reduction in copper supply and potential disruption to production and exports. Traders would likely price in tighter physical availability and supply risks for copper over coming weeks.

Golden Metal Resources announced overlimit assay results from its Garfield project in Nevada, including high-grade copper occurrences (up to 4.27% Cu in reported overlimit samples) alongside gold and silver. Peru’s copper output increased 1.9% in October to 240,097 tonnes year-on-year, driven mainly by a nearly 36% rise at Glencore-controlled Antapaccay and ~7% growth at Southern Copper. A geotechnical program is also underway. | Copper prices rose in London as a weaker dollar improved currency buying power and tighter supply prospects next year were reinforced by recent mine closures/disruptions. Morgan Stanley notes China’s copper and aluminium output is at record highs but inventories/trade suggest absorption rather than buildup, implying solid domestic demand. While positive for potential future discovery, the release provides no production or supply-change details. | Copper rallied sharply, gaining 2.8% to around $8,565/t on Thursday, the biggest daily jump in nearly a year, driven by a weaker dollar and expectations of Fed rate cuts. With demand from EVs and renewables still strong, analysts warn of a move toward deficit and higher prices. | Workers at Antofagasta’s Centinela copper mine in Chile voted to approve a new labor contract, ending the risk of a strike. The company reports high-grade NiEq from a drill hole and plans expanded drilling in 2024, improving prospects for future copper and nickel supply. | Stillwater Critical Minerals says drilling at its Stillwater West (Montana) intercepted new “N series” sulfide mineralization, including 2.89% nickel-equivalent over 400.8m, and copper-nickel sulfides. London copper was steady after a sharp prior-session jump as dovish Fed expectations pressured the dollar near four-month lows. Three-month LME copper was almost flat, while January SHFE copper rose. The restricted list includes copper, nickel and aluminium. Markets reacted with sharp overnight moves: palladium jumped nearly 12% and copper logged its largest intraday gain in 11 months, indicating immediate supply-risk pricing. | Jubilee Metals Group plc said it plans to raise £10m to support copper expansion. The funding plan implies investment toward increasing copper production capacity, which can lift market expectations for future copper supply and improve near-term sentiment around copper prices. | Mitsubishi Materials says it has initiated procedures to qualify for the Copper Mark Assurance Framework at its Naoshima and Onahama copper smelter and refinery sites, with qualification expected in fiscal 2025. The company highlights responsible production efforts, renewable energy use, and expanded E-scrap processing capacity, aimed at stable electrolytic copper supply. | Jubilee Metals plans to raise GBP10 million to fund its “Green Copper” project in Zambia, following acquisition of a large copper waste-rock asset and a partnership with International Resources Holding. The company reports surface sampling of ore-grade copper and plans EM survey and reverse-circulation drilling, supporting bullish sentiment for future copper supply. | Li3 Lithium says it is prioritizing its 100%-owned Northern Lights copper project in Nevada and plans an accelerated EM and reverse-circulation drilling program to define grade and thickness. Surface sampling reports ore-grade copper over up to 4 meters width, supporting further development potential. | Arizona Sonoran Copper (ASCU) signed an option agreement with Nuton (Rio Tinto subsidiary) to form a JV for the Cactus copper project in Arizona. The tests aim to improve extraction rates and advance toward a PFS. | Jubilee Metals raised GBP13 million via a share placing, with net proceeds earmarked to fund its Zambian copper operations. Management plans to accelerate a newly acquired copper waste rock project, expand sulphide recovery at its Sable refinery, and progress related Mufulira slag development and working capital. | British Columbia’s EAO ruled that the Copper Mountain mine expansion near Princeton does not require a full environmental review because additional land disturbance is below the legal threshold, despite Indigenous and environmental objections. The permitting will proceed via the Major Mines Office, extending mine life while not implying immediate supply shocks. | Panama’s MICI reported that after a court ruling voiding parts of the mining contract, an order was sent to the Cobre Panamá company on December 5 to end operations, covering extraction, processing, transport, export and marketing. Copper futures opened lower as a steadier U.S. dollar amid uncertainty about the Fed’s rate path pressured prices. Three-month LME copper fell and Shanghai January copper dipped slightly. Brokers (via Longview and UBS) forecast near-term copper price weakness, arguing the metal is elevated versus indicators of Chinese activity. The initiative supports construction and logistics for the SuperGrid connection, expected to boost near-term copper-intensive electrical infrastructure demand and, secondarily, other grid metals like aluminium. | Copper prices edged up on Friday as the dollar retreated, but Reuters notes the contract is on track for a fourth straight weekly decline amid weak Chinese economic data and concerns over rate cuts. Aluminium and nickel also moved modestly, reflecting broader FX/macro sentiment. | Copper prices in London rebounded on Friday, supported by oversold technical signals and lower LME on-warrant copper stocks hitting a four-month low after cancellations. Gains are tempered by cautious trading ahead of Chinese New Year and a firmer dollar. Aluminium and nickel also ticked up alongside copper. | The article says efforts to remove rare-earth magnets from EV motors can raise copper use by shifting toward induction and wound-rotor, magnet-free designs with more copper windings. It also discusses winding-efficiency improvements and limited aluminum substitution, but expects copper demand for EV motors to grow strongly through 2034. | Benton Resources reported additional trenching/prospecting results from its Great Burnt copper-gold project in Newfoundland, including grab samples up to 4.35% copper near AEM conductors. Copper prices rose 0.3% on the LME and 0.7% on the SHFE as the U.S. dollar weakened ahead of major central bank meetings. The softer dollar makes greenback-priced metals cheaper. It can earn majority stakes by funding exploration over 24 months, with further upside from additional drilling. The move is exploration-led, with no immediate production impact. | Surge Copper finalized the acquisition of a 100% stake in the Berg Property in British Columbia from Thompson Creek Metals. The program is aimed at updating an open pit mine plan and slope stability, without any immediate production or supply-impact guidance. | Probe Gold reported expanded copper mineralization at its La Peltrie project, including 0.13% Cu over 513.5 metres and higher-grade intercepts up to 1.17% Cu over 9.5 metres. The release notes further survey plans and management’s bullish view on nickel and copper prices.

True North Copper has signed a binding offtake and toll-mining deal with Glencore to toll treat up to one million tonnes per year of copper concentrate from its Cloncurry project and give TNC full access to concentrate processing. He highlights a $15,000/tonne level needed for new investment. | Barrick Gold has fast-tracked the Super Pit expansion at its Lumwana copper mine in Zambia, targeting first copper production in 2028 and around 240,000 tonnes annually. Copper strengthened overnight as China unveiled banking liquidity support and better US activity improved demand expectations. Phoenix Copper has launched an equity offer to raise up to GBP2.4 million to fund mining and processing equipment for its Empire open-pit copper (and silver) project in Idaho. Copper opened lower as a steady dollar and softer demand expectations from China weighed on sentiment ahead of U.S. data. Three-month copper on the LME slid 0.2% and Shanghai prices were flat. Zijin Mining reported over 1 million tonnes of mined copper in 2023 and is expanding major projects, supporting a tighter copper supply outlook. | Xanadu says recent drilling at Mongolia’s Kharmagtai project, shared with Zijin, expanded higher-grade copper-gold mineralisation at White Hill and improved Golden Eagle resource grade. The update mainly affects expected copper concentrate sales, but likely at small market scale. | Benton Resources said infill drill holes at its Great Burnt copper project in Newfoundland returned high-grade intercepts, including 5.81% Cu over 24.0 m, improving and expanding the deposit’s grade and width. Residents in Lisbon Valley oppose expansion of the Lisbon Valley Copper Mine, citing fears that in-situ leaching could threaten drinking-water wells and that hydrology and monitoring are insufficient. Sentiment also weighed on metals as smelters were urged to cut production amid tighter raw-material supplies, partially supporting prices. | SMM says copper concentrate TCs are extremely low, prompting concern about smelter output. The move signals reduced spot market activity for copper. | Volta Energy Solutions (VES) plans to expand its presence in the North American EV market, leveraging its technology in ultra-thin copper foil used in battery electrodes. The article cites rapid EV growth and rising expected copper-foil demand. It supports copper demand expectations over the coming weeks. | Michigan’s Strategic Fund is considering a $50M performance-based grant to support Highland Copper’s proposed Copperwood copper mine in the Upper Peninsula. Highland Copper says its Wakefield/Ironwood mine is in reserve status with drilling and geology completed, permitting and a feasibility study done, and the project nearing financing after about $35m in treasury. The update is administrative and does not signal immediate supply changes for nickel or copper. | Glencore said 2023 copper output fell to 1.01 million tonnes and nickel output to 97,600 tonnes. It cut its 2024 copper outlook to 950,000–1.01 million tonnes and expects nickel output of about 80,000–90,000 tonnes, reflecting mine changes and lower volumes. | Glencore said 2023 copper output fell to 1.01 million tonnes and reduced 2024 expectations to 950,000–1.01 million tonnes after the Cobar mine sale. The company flags further falls. | Intrepid Metals announced a drill contract and mobilization for a 5,000 m program at its Corral Copper Property in Arizona, with drilling expected to start within two weeks. Telstra says copper cable thefts from telecommunications sites in Australia’s Hunter wine region caused weeks of mobile, phone and internet outages. Services resumed after replacements, but copper theft remains an ongoing issue. | Telstra reports copper cable thefts in Australia’s Hunter wine region have disrupted mobile and some fixed-line internet and phone services. It reports nearly 1,000 security incidents since January 2022, with copper involved in 87%. EV Minerals reports additional historical drill results and database capture for its EV Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Quebec, highlighting near-surface nickel and copper mineralization and planning additional drilling as assays return. Copper prices were mixed as tight supply concerns outweighed demand uncertainty. LME copper eased slightly while SHFE copper hit record highs on expectations of potential smelter output cuts in China. China’s industrial output and retail sales rose, but real estate remained weak. Aluminium and nickel also drifted lower. | The piece urges Michigan to oppose taxpayer-funded support for the proposed Copperwood copper mine near Lake Superior and a state park. Freeport CEO Richard Adkerson urged U.S. lawmakers and regulators to improve the mine permitting process to move projects from verbal acceptance to approvals, without easing environmental standards.
