Peru’s copper production fell 1.2% in January year-on-year to 205,375 tonnes. The decline followed a 17% drop at MMG’s Las Bambas and a 13.4% fall at Freeport-McMoRan’s Cerro Verde. The article cites ministry data and compares it with higher December output. | Brantford council is set to approve a bylaw targeting scrap-metal theft, restricting dealers from certain metals and banning cash transactions. The measure tightens salvage yard licensing, bans cash transactions, and requires traceable payments and documentation. Officials say theft losses have neared $1M over two years, mainly affecting copper tubing, piping and wiring. | Brantford council plans to approve a bylaw restricting transactions for certain metals, including copper tubing, piping, and wiring, and banning cash dealings in salvage yard sales. Restrictions aim to improve traceability via cheques or e-transfers. | Susan McDonald urges adding copper to Australia’s critical minerals list to protect supply chains and unlock financing and international investment. The release provides no specific near-term production or supply changes. | Copper Fox reported Q1 2024 financials and ongoing copper-focused exploration at projects including Van Dyke, Schaft Creek, Mineral Mountain and Sombrero Butte. BSE/ICCL informed members that an ICCL accredited warehouse is available for deposit and delivery of copper (and zinc) under contract specifications. The update is primarily about settlement/warehouse logistics rather than a fundamental copper supply-demand change. | International Resources Holding’s 80% stake in Zambia’s Lubambe copper project is up for sale, with UAE oil-major-linked investor IRH seeking to outbid or complicate JCHX’s January purchase deal. The deal competition highlights intensified African copper securing efforts, potentially tightening near-term supply expectations. | Investorideas.com publishes a podcast interview with Chakana Copper CEO/geologist David Kelley. The discussion centers on a fully funded drill program beginning in April for the Soledad Project in Peru, emphasizing high-grade copper-gold-silver mineralization and investor interest. The news is supportive for copper sentiment via exploration upside. | Lifezone Metals says Tanzania issued a licence for its Kahama multi-metal processing facility, allowing refining of nickel and copper (and cobalt) from the Kabanga Nickel Project using hydromet technology. EV Minerals reported historic drill targets and selected intercepts from its EV Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Quebec, including multi-metre grades of nickel and copper and plans for follow-up drilling after assay results. Queensland’s government has granted Eva Copper Mine near Cloncurry priority status, allowing the Coordinator-General to coordinate remaining approvals. Bell links theft incentives to world copper prices and calls for stronger laws and recycler controls. | UK boat owners and builders are being urged to switch sacrificial anodes from zinc to aluminium. The piece argues copper’s rally is just beginning, citing China-related supply catalysts. Banks like ING, UBS and Macquarie forecast continued supply deficits and rising copper prices. | LME and SHFE copper prices opened lower but recovered slightly, closing near $8,863/mt and 72,340 yuan/mt. Troops guard the site, but disruption risk persists. | CNBC’s Chart Investing piece argues copper is supported by robust demand tied to clean energy and AI-related electrification, alongside supply tightness from mining and South America geopolitical barriers. Fund managers remain bullish on copper despite some profit-taking as the CME squeeze dissipates. COTR data show large net long positions on both CME and LME, with only modest trimming. Momentum has faded and prices consolidated above $10,000/t, suggesting limited immediate directional pressure. | Copper prices fell after a strong rally as weak physical demand in China weighed on the market. LME three-month copper slipped below $10,000 and SHFE July copper dropped, while SHFE and LME copper stocks rose. Aluminium and nickel also eased, suggesting broadly bearish sentiment in base metals. | Copper prices in London edged up but stayed below $10,000/t due to weak physical demand in China. Rising LME and SHFE inventories, negative import premiums/discounts, and softer Chinese manufacturing weighed on sentiment. While supply-disruption risks were noted, analysts said they were largely priced in and strike effects should be short-lived. | The article’s headline says China’s unusually large copper stockpiles are building, raising concerns about demand. Elevated inventories in major consuming hubs often imply slower offtake and greater near-term availability, which can weigh on copper prices in the coming days to weeks. | C3 Metals reported long drill intercepts at its Bellas Gate, Jamaica project, including 294m at 0.30% copper from surface at the Camel Hill prospect, plus additional copper-gold mineralization at Geo Hill. Results are used to update models and plan more drilling, boosting near-term copper sentiment. | Greenwave reported record May 2024 revenues from its downstream metal processing system, citing higher pricing and increased volumes. The release attributes part of copper price strength to strong demand tied to electric vehicles, renewables, and AI. It also notes its Kelford facility recovers copper from shred residue. | Benton Resources reports successful initial Phase 3 drilling at the Great Burnt copper deposit, including metres of semi-massive/massive sulphides with visible chalcopyrite, and ongoing trenching at the South Pond area. Reported historical highlights show high copper grades, supporting continued exploration expansion plans. | Copper prices near a one-month low as real demand fails to match strong electrification-driven expectations. Three-month LME copper slipped below $10,000, with sellers paying China to destock. Funds reportedly sold copper and bought aluminium. Aluminium was also down on the day; nickel fell alongside broader metals weakness. | BHP and the union at Chile’s Spence copper mine will begin five days of government-mediated talks to avert a strike after workers rejected a new contract. The mine is operating normally during mediation, but if no agreement is reached, a strike could start next Wednesday. | Ironbark Zinc will acquire an 80% stake in two copper exploration projects in Queensland (Simon and Anderson) from Aeon Metals, paying $100,000 with due diligence over 30 days. The company plans initial data compilation, mapping, and sampling near Mt Isa, but the deal is exploratory and small. | Norilsk Nickel is upgrading its Nadezhda Metallurgical Plant in Norilsk, including replacing a flash smelting furnace and implementing a Sulfur Program to cut SO2 emissions. The work uses demolition robots and spider cranes to improve safety and efficiency, and management expects production volumes to rise after repairs. | Armenia’s National Statistics Committee reports copper concentrate production rose 37.2% year-on-year to 105,160 tonnes in January–April 2024. The increase implies higher concentrate availability versus last year, which may dampen near-term copper price expectations. Zinc and molybdenum output moved in different directions. | Alaska Energy Metals said TSX Venture Exchange accepted an option agreement granting it the right to acquire 100% of the Bambino nickel-copper property in western Quebec and will commence exploration programs. The release is an early-stage acquisition/exploration update without any near-term production or supply impact. | Alaska Energy Metals said TSX-V accepted an option agreement giving it the right to acquire 100% of the Bambino nickel-copper property in western Quebec and to start exploration. The update is about future project development, with no immediate production or inventory impact for nickel or copper. | RCMP is investigating several thefts of copper wire from substations and power poles near Whitbourne, Newfoundland. Incidents occurred on multiple dates in May, with grounding wires severed. Authorities believe the cases are connected as utilities face replacement needs following the vandalism.

Copper futures rebounded above $10,000/t as the dollar softened on expectations of Fed easing and as signs of firmer China demand emerged. LME three-month copper rose 1.6%. LME aluminum and nickel also advanced, with Reuters citing renewed copper buying activity in China. | Silver One says it found additional copper and silver showings at its Phoenix Silver Project in Arizona, with select samples up to 7.79% Cu. The area is near Freeport McMoRan’s Miami copper mine and BHP’s Ocelot porphyry project, and drilling is targeted for Q3 2024. | Copper rebounded above $10,000/t as investors increased bets on U.S. rate cuts after easing labour-market data, lifting demand sentiment and weakening the dollar. LME three-month copper rose 1.5% and Shanghai July copper gained 1.2%. The market also cited tightening concentrate shortages and selective buying after recent declines. | Trafigura says copper’s recent spike is not backed by fundamentals. The comment is likely to affect trader sentiment and short-term expectations for copper, potentially encouraging profit-taking if the market is viewed as overextended. The piece appears to be commentary rather than a direct supply-demand development. | Copper prices rebounded 1% to around $10,027/ton ahead of the ECB’s expected first rate cut, supported by optimism that lower rates may improve growth. Gains were capped by concerns over sluggish demand, especially in China where inventories rose and producer operating rates fell. LME aluminium and nickel also rose. | Kodiak Copper Corp. upsized a non-brokered private placement to about $6.65 million, issuing flow-through and non-flow-through units and warrants. Proceeds will fund working capital and Canadian exploration expenses for its copper projects. The release is primarily equity-financing related, with no stated immediate effect on copper supply. | Fastmarkets reports copper concentrate TCs for Asia Pacific staying deeply negative, reflecting tight concentrate supply amid mine closures, weaker output, and smelter capacity expansions. It ties this upstream tightness to recent all-time highs in LME copper and argues concentrate constraints may translate into refined supply pressure despite contract benchmarks. | Nevada Copper reported depleted cash and the absence of binding or adequate financing to continue operations at its Pumpkin Hollow underground mine. The company has started scaling down operations and may seek creditor protection. It also notes director resignations tied to Pala. Funding constraints could limit copper production and supply ramp-up. | Surge Copper Corp. announced a non-brokered $1.6M flow-through financing to fund critical-mineral exploration at its Berg project, aiming to intensify drilling/testing of priority targets toward a PFS. The company also referenced prior hard-dollar financing and ongoing reconnaissance work, signaling continued copper supply development. | Sprott Physical Copper Trust closed an IPO of 10,000,000 units at US$10 each, raising about US$100 million. The trust is designed to hold physical copper, and net proceeds will generally be used to purchase copper, creating incremental near-term buying interest that can support copper prices. | Hudbay and several unions signed a letter of intent to enter a Project Labor Agreement for constructing the Copper World Mine in Arizona, including plans to produce “Made in America” copper cathode. The project is positioned as a domestic copper supply-chain contributor, supporting sentiment toward future copper output. | Nevada Copper has significantly reduced operations at its Pumpkin Hollow mine after it failed to secure funding and saw two board members resign from its leading investor. The company’s shares fell sharply. The operational scaling down signals near-term supply risk for copper, which can pressure copper prices.

London copper eased as softer-than-expected physical demand from China weighed on prices. Markets also awaited U.S. jobs data for guidance on when Fed rate cuts may begin, lifting expectations of cuts after recent labor-market cooling signals. Other LME metals, including aluminium and nickel, were modestly higher. | China’s unwrought copper imports rose 15.8% year over year in May to 514,000 metric tons, up from 444,010 tons in May 2023, according to customs data. The report indicates higher inbound copper supply into the world’s top consumer, but offers no additional drivers for price direction. | China’s May unwrought copper imports rose 15.8% year-on-year to 514,000 tonnes, beating expectations despite weak physical consumption. Imports were up 17.4% from April. Analysts cited potential additional Russia-origin flows into China following Western sanctions in April, with DRC shipments also improving. | London copper fell after mixed China trade data. Better exports contrasted with slower imports and higher-than-expected unwrought copper imports, raising concern that Chinese inventories will grow; SHFE stocks hit a four-year high. Broader macro cues from an upcoming US jobs report and Fed cut expectations also influenced pricing; aluminium and nickel moved mildly. | Copper on the LME is poised for a third weekly decline, breaking below $10,000/t after mixed Chinese trade data. Better exports but slower import growth and higher unwrought copper imports raise concerns about rising inventories. Traders also await a U.S. jobs report for rate-cut timing, weighing on growth-linked metals. | London copper is set for a third weekly decline and has slipped below the $10,000 per metric ton threshold, with three-month LME copper down about 2% to roughly $9,950. Moves are attributed to mixed China trade data, pointing to softer near-term demand expectations. | Surge Copper upsized its private placement to about C$2.2M (charity flow-through and non-flow-through shares) to fund Canadian exploration expenditures for its Berg and Ootsa copper projects. The release is mainly a capital markets announcement and does not indicate immediate changes to copper supply or demand. | Abacus Mining & Exploration agreed to acquire full ownership of Almadex’s Willow porphyry copper property in Nevada, pending exchange approval. The deal involves issuing shares and a future top-up provision, with Abacus required to drill a 600m exploration hole by end-2025. Prior work indicates Cu-Mo mineralisation and an NSR royalty. | Chile’s Codelco reported April copper output of 95,100 mt, down 6.1% year-on-year, according to Cochilco data. Output also fell at Escondida, while Collahuasi rose 1.9%. The production drop from key Chilean supply sources is likely to pressure short-term copper sentiment lower. | Zambia plans for KoBold to spend about $2.3 billion on a large new copper mine. The announcement highlights major capital deployment toward copper production capacity in Zambia, which typically boosts market sentiment about future output and helps support copper price expectations over the next few weeks.

Japan is seeing more copper cable thefts at solar power plants as global copper prices have surged. Authorities and local governments are considering ordinances to license and restrict metal buyers. Police report thousands of theft cases in several prefectures, with increased buyer demand for stolen copper wires. | Copper fell to its lowest in over five weeks on a firm dollar and shifting expectations for Fed rate cuts after stronger U.S. jobs data. Physical demand constraints were noted, including a continued discount in China import premiums. Aluminium declined about 1%; nickel was nearly unchanged. | Copper rebounded from five-week lows, rising 0.6% after U.S. jobs data-driven selloff shifted focus to improving fundamentals. Traders expected support from China returning from holiday and cited better China PMI activity, while concerns remain from rising warehouse inventories and weaker Yangshan premium. Aluminium and nickel saw only modest moves. | Nevada Copper Corp. filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after failing to secure additional interim funding. The company has requested court approval for $60 million debtor-in-possession financing to provide liquidity and has indicated it does not expect to continue operations, aiming to preserve assets during restructuring. | Copper rebounded 0.6% after dipping to the lowest since May 2, supported by shifting focus to improving fundamentals and expected Tuesday buying as China returns from holiday. The prior sell-off was triggered by strong US jobs data and a stronger dollar. China stocks remain high and premiums weak. Aluminium and nickel saw modest declines/gains. | Power Nickel begins a 2024 summer drilling program of 20 holes and over 8,000 metres at its Nisk property in Quebec, targeting extensions of high-grade polymetallic sulphide lenses. The program focuses on both the polymetallic Lion Zone (PGE/Pd/Au/Ag/Cu) and nickel-rich Nisk Main. No production or shipments are mentioned. | Nevada Copper has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection and will halt its Pumpkin Hollow mine as part of a restructuring process. The company has replaced its CEO as part of management changes. The mine stoppage implies reduced near-term copper output, which can pressure copper prices. | The piece centers on a copper miner going bankrupt even as copper prices remain near record highs. The implication is worsening financial stress for the producer, prompting markets to reassess near-term supply outlook and the viability of high-cost output, which can weigh on copper sentiment in the coming days. | Nexans and other cable makers are increasing copper recycling by converting more scrap into copper rods, aiming to raise recycled content. The piece links higher copper demand to electrification and data centers, while noting some aluminum smelters have reduced output due to higher electricity prices, boosting copper’s relative appeal. | Copper is seeing strong near-term downside after tightening earlier in the year. The LME price reportedly fell to $9,652/ton (down 11.5%), breaking an ascending trend line as China’s economic data disappointed and speculative flows reversed. Mine-closure supply tightness is noted but framed as eventual. | Pacific Ridge Exploration closed the first tranche of financing, raising C$1.68 million, to fund general working capital and a diamond drill program at its Chuchi copper-gold project in British Columbia. The company plans to drill 2,400 meters, beginning mid-August, with a second tranche expected shortly. | Codelco’s copper output has been impacted by lingering effects of a rock collapse, suggesting temporary supply losses at a key mine/refining source. Reduced production can tighten the near-term copper market balance and increase expectations for higher prices over the coming days and weeks.

Base metals fell as the yuan weakened, reducing Chinese buying power. LME/SHFE copper dropped, while aluminium eased amid high inventories, weak physical demand, and a wider LME cash-to-three-month discount reflecting non-tight nearby supply. Nickel moved less consistently, amid broader downside pressure. | Mumbai police arrested five men for stealing ₹6–7 lakh worth of copper wires from underground utility cables near Dadar–Matunga. MTNL reported over 400 telephone lines tripped and cited 105 metres of copper wire stolen. Authorities say thieves dug the footpath nightly to extract and sell the copper as scrap. | Nornickel is reconstructing the pyrometallurgical line at its Nadezhda Smelter by dismantling flash smelting furnace No. 2 and installing a new unit. The smelter processes nickel concentrates and produces copper-nickel matte sent for further processing. The repair is planned to take ~60 days while improving reliability. | Record commodity prices are attributed partly to the U.S. economy running hot and possibly insufficiently tight policy. The piece notes copper hit record highs last month, and discusses how Fed expectations, interest rates, and the dollar can move commodity prices via demand and currency effects, supporting near-term upside risk for copper. | Orecap updates on Cuprum’s Thierry copper project in Ontario, highlighting recent flow-through financing, commissioning of remaining assays from Summer 2023, completion of an updated NI 43-101, and historical resource/PEA details. The project’s CuEq calculations assume copper and nickel prices, but no new supply or production impact is indicated. | Copper prices hit a seven-week low as the weaker yuan reduced purchasing power for China and as LME copper inventories rose after fresh inflows. The piece also describes a contango structure with no imminent shortages, while aluminium and nickel fell amid broader base-metal pressure. | BNN Bloomberg highlights that Freeport-McMoRan’s CEO says the company is searching for ways to expand copper production. The prospect of additional output supports near-term bullish sentiment for copper by improving expectations for supply, though details on timing and realized incremental production are not provided in the excerpt.

BHP said it and workers at the Spence copper mine in Chile have agreed to extend contract negotiations, pushing off the risk of a strike. The delay reduces near-term disruption to copper production and thus is supportive for expectations of copper supply over the coming days and weeks. | The piece reviews copper’s pullback after a sharp rally, citing prior drivers like better-than-expected growth, short covering, and mine disruption in Panama, while projecting a near-term range and medium-term tightening. It also notes strong ETF inflows and discusses copper ETF options, with brief substitution to aluminium if prices spike. | Copper rebounded as the dollar steadied ahead of U.S. CPI and the Fed decision. Traders cited expectations of a dovish statement and lower currency pressure supporting dollar-priced commodities. LME copper rose 0.8% from recent lows, while aluminium was slightly lower and nickel flat, reflecting broad industrial-metal reaction. | Los Angeles reports ongoing copper wiring thefts from the Sixth Street Bridge, stripping miles of cable and disabling lighting. A city task force has not eliminated the issue, with repairs estimated at about $2 million for this bridge and over $17 million citywide last year. Officials plan to harden infrastructure against repeat thefts. | Thieves across the U.S. are cutting and stealing EV charging cables, using bolt cutters to remove copper-containing wiring. The story highlights that copper prices are near record levels (about $5.20/lb), making scrap theft profitable and causing chargers to go offline, pushing firms to add security. | Kodiak Copper Corp. begins a 2024 exploration program at its 100% owned MPD copper-gold porphyry project in southern British Columbia, mobilizing two drill rigs and planning 10,000 metres of drilling alongside 3D IP and soil geochemical surveys to advance drill-ready targets. No near-term production or supply disruption is mentioned. | Ero Copper announced it received the final operational license for its Tucumã copper project in Pará, with construction ~99% complete and commissioning well advanced. Pre-stripping and full mining have started, and first concentrate is expected early in Q3 2024, improving near-term copper supply prospects. | A ResearchAndMarkets report highlights growth in the global copper foil market, driven by electronics demand, 5G infrastructure, EV growth, and copper foil applications in PCBs, batteries, shielding, transformers, and energy storage. It is a market study with forecasts through 2032 and no explicit short-term supply or trading disruption details. | District Copper Corp. reports adding three new claims to its Copper Keg porphyry copper project in British Columbia, increasing the land package and stating it now controls the porphyry target area and associated anomalies. The company plans additional exploration work, with no production or near-term supply changes mentioned. | Freeport-McMoRan says it will boost copper output using new processing technology to recover copper it previously couldn’t access, without opening new mines. The company aims to add 800 million pounds annually over three to five years, starting with 200 million pounds more now, amid an existing copper shortage and high prices. | South Africa’s Copper Development Agency warns of a “copper catastrophe” driven by infrastructure theft and scrap dealers exporting stolen copper at high profits. Legitimate users struggle to source legally, as stolen cable and other infrastructure are smelted into ingots/billets and shipped out, tightening effective supply. | Green Light Metals entered a 12-month exclusive option agreement to purchase interests in the Cerro Colorado property in Arizona, described as a copper porphyry target within the Laramide porphyry belt. The deal involves issuing shares/tranches and potential future cash or shares for closing, supporting copper exploration outlook. | Norilsk Nickel says sanctions have cut revenues and disrupted payments, deliveries and pricing, limiting copper plant sulfur-equipment upgrades. Separately, the US and UK have banned Russian copper, nickel and aluminium from LME/CME trading. Norilsk plans to move some copper smelting to China later, but the trading bans are immediate. | Coppernico Metals said it has hired a Peru-based drilling contractor to start an initial 3,000+ metre diamond drilling program at its Sombrero copper-gold project in Ayacucho, Peru, with mobilization scheduled for July 2024. The update is exploratory with no production guidance, implying limited near-term copper price impact. | Nevada Copper’s U.S. subsidiary filed for Chapter 11 and the firm is seeking a $60 million DIP package while exploring a sale. With mining and milling suspended at its Pumpkin Hollow copper mine and severe liquidity stress, near-term output prospects weaken, adding supply-risk sentiment. Copper’s recent highs are noted. | Ero Copper received the operational license for its Tucumã copper project, with construction ~99% complete and commissioning underway. First concentrate remains expected in early Q3 2024 as flotation and milling commissioning nears completion. The milestone reduces regulatory risk and supports near-term expectations for copper output ramp-up.

The piece highlights that a Chinese trader’s reported $20 million pile of Russian copper has gone missing. While details are limited, the implication is reduced nearby physical copper availability, which can add short-term tightness and support prices, though the overall market-wide impact is likely limited. | Reuters columnist says China’s unusual copper stockpile build will work through global balances. With ShFE inflows continuing when they normally reverse, weak property/manufacturing demand and robust imports point to oversupply. Imports of refined copper are expected to ease and surplus to reach world markets, stabilizing but around lower levels near $10,000/t. | Copper prices fall as hawkish comments from Federal Reserve officials undermine market expectations for faster rate cuts. The sell-off reflects tighter financial conditions and weaker sentiment toward industrial commodities, causing the prior rally to lose momentum and pressuring copper lower over the near term. | Copper thieves have stripped miles of wiring from Los Angeles’ 6th Street Bridge, disabling LED lighting and leaving the viaduct “completely in the dark.” City officials cite thousands of cases statewide and millions in repair costs, and propose enforcement and task forces to curb copper theft and scrap sales. | Copper prices paused after rebounding, pressured by the Fed’s delayed rate-cut timeline, which steadied the U.S. dollar. Reuters notes profit-taking and highlights rising LME inventories in Taiwan and South Korea. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same macro-driven risk/FX move. | Queensland authorities report a sharp rise in copper cable theft, with up to 1,000 metres taken in a night and repair bills exceeding A$4.5m annually. Cable is allegedly sold to scrap dealers, while some network sites replace stolen copper with aluminium, shifting demand. | Romios Gold reports new satellite-image reinterpretation identifying >1 km sericitic alteration zones and additional linear targets on its Kinkaid Au-Cu-Ag property in Nevada. Follow-up field work begins June 14. Boulders along a stream bed returned up to 13.3% Cu, supporting a possible buried porphyry copper system. | District Copper Corp. begins Phase I field work at its Copper Keg project in British Columbia, including mobilizing crews and contracting soil sampling, with possible follow-on geophysics. The project targets porphyry-style copper mineralization based on prior mapping and assays, but no production or supply change is announced. | Idaho Copper received TruScan XRF scanning results from the first six drillholes at its Idaho CuMo deposit. The mobile XRF system measured copper and molybdenum grades at 1.5 cm intervals to support ore sorting, potentially reducing waste and enabling a smaller mill. The update is exploratory and not a direct market supply shock. | GT Resources completed a C$1.84m non-brokered private placement (flow-through shares) and mobilized exploration drill rigs for the Canalask copper-nickel project in the Yukon. The program targets EM Maxwell plate and downhole surveys to locate massive sulphide mineralization, including historical nickel and up to 6.1% copper assays. | A trader reports a US$20m hit after a copper cargo vanishes. The loss implies copper supply has been disrupted or delayed, reducing nearby availability. In the first-days horizon, missing cargoes can increase expectations of tighter supply and support upward pressure in copper prices. | U.S. auto makers are reacting to a rise in copper prices, citing higher input costs because copper is critical for conventional and especially EV vehicles. The article also notes strong aluminum price performance. It adds that the Baltimore port resuming operations should ease supply-chain bottlenecks, supporting near-term demand sentiment.

Nevada Copper has filed for Chapter 11 and will lay off 117 workers. The restructuring and cost-cutting raise concerns about continuity of copper mining and near-term production levels, creating uncertainty around supply and potentially weighing on copper prices in the coming days. | China’s Wuchan Zhongda is investigating missing refined copper worth about $15 million after a cargo from Russia allegedly failed to arrive. The loss (~110 million yuan) is described as a small share of profit. Reuters also notes higher Russia-linked copper imports into China earlier in 2024. | Copper prices fell as a stronger dollar and rising LME-registered inventories signaled a supply surplus, widening contango. Nickel dropped to its lowest in over two months, pressured by higher LME and SHFE stocks and increased China high-grade cathode output. Aluminium also eased amid the same bearish complex tone. | Reuters reports copper prices slipping and nickel hitting a two-month low, driven by a firmer U.S. dollar and rising LME inventories suggesting a supply surplus. Copper LME stocks jumped 28% in a month. Nickel is pressured by high warehouse stocks and China cathode output increases. Aluminium also fell. | Cooler US inflation is portrayed as strengthening expectations for Fed rate cuts, which would weaken the dollar and broadly support commodities. The piece specifically ties this to copper, contrasting an earlier decline tied to hawkish guidance with the later “rate cuts/looser policy” bullish case for copper. | Inflection Resources selected Fleet Space’s ExoSphere to run ambient noise tomography surveys to map and refine copper-gold exploration targets across Australia’s Macquarie Arc. New priority drill targets at Duck Creek prompted AngloGold Ashanti to upgrade the project phase to include drilling, supporting future copper supply prospects but not current output. | The piece argues copper is in a “supercycle,” citing protectionist moves and a widening demand-supply deficit from energy transition and AI. It references copper production constraints (Gibraltar strike suspension), output ramp-up (Teck’s QB extension), permitting progress (Ero’s Tucumã), and processing tech (Freeport leaching) to expand copper supply. | BHP copper miners accepted a wage offer, easing labor tensions in Chile. The development reduces the probability of work stoppages or disruptions at copper operations, which typically lowers the immediate supply risk premium. As a result, copper prices may face mild downward pressure over the coming days. | A union at BHP’s Spence copper mine in Chile accepted a new contract proposal, averting a potential strike after an earlier tentative agreement required a member vote. With the strike risk removed, near-term production disruption fears ease, which can support copper price sentiment shortly. | A union representing workers at BHP’s Spence copper mine in Chile confirms acceptance of a new contract. The announcement suggests labor tensions are eased, lowering the likelihood of operational disruption. That can support expectations for more stable near-term copper output and supply conditions.

China’s primary aluminium production rose 7.2% in May to the highest level in nearly a decade, with output also up year-to-date. Producers cited strong demand growth prospects for solar and EVs and speculative buying. LME and Shanghai aluminium prices hit multi-month highs, implying bullish near-term pressure. | Copper prices opened lower as China’s May industrial output missed forecasts, reinforcing worries that the weak property sector will limit demand for industrial metals. A firm U.S. dollar also weighed on copper. The article notes prior speculative-driven May highs followed by downward corrections. | Golden Mile Resources signed a binding term sheet to acquire a 51% earn-in for the Pearl Copper Project in Arizona, with total potential investment of A$12m and an NSR royalty covering copper production. Payments are linked to a JORC-compliant copper resource target, with further options and JV arrangements. | Copper fell to its lowest in almost eight weeks as weak Chinese industrial data clouded demand expectations. LME three-month copper slid to about $9,631/ton and LME copper stocks rose. The same China-focused demand worries coincided with declines in aluminium and nickel prices. | Riverside Resources completed acquisition and title transfer of the Maria Luisa copper property adjacent to its Ariel project in Sonora, Mexico, consolidating a larger, fully permitted drill target near the La Caridad copper mine. The release is focused on exploration readiness, not production or discovered reserves, limiting near-term price impact. | Record CME copper prices are prompting Chinese buyers to defer U.S. copper scrap shipments because U.S. scrap is priced at a discount to CME/LME benchmarks. China’s concentrate shortages have boosted scrap imports overall, but higher prices are slowing U.S. flow, affecting nearby physical supply dynamics. | The piece is an interview with Lifezone Metals’ CEO about the company’s Hydromet technology and its Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania. It claims the process could unlock new LME-grade nickel and also copper supply, but offers no near-term operational updates. A feasibility study is due by Q3 2024. | Zijin Mining’s subsidiary Duobaoshan Copper announced a discovery at the Tongshan Copper Mine that adds 3.65 million tonnes of copper reserves from deep ore bodies, confirmed by Heilongjiang authorities. Total project reserves rise to over 5.6 million tonnes of copper, with some associated molybdenum, gold and silver. | Kobrea Exploration starts field crews at the Upland Copper Project in British Columbia, conducting soil geochemical and passive seismic surveys to infill historical copper mineralization and assess overburden depth. Historical drilling shows copper-rich intervals and expanding targets, but the release provides no immediate production or supply changes. | Tempus Resources completes the acquisition of the Prescott copper-base metals project in Nunavut, planning an initial airborne gravity survey and subsequent mapping and sampling. The program targets sediment-hosted copper and Mississippi Valley-type zinc-lead deposits. Permitting is underway and exploration is expected to start this month. | Copper is falling to an eight-week low following additional soft economic data from China. The negative data likely reinforces concerns about slower industrial activity and construction demand in the world’s major consumer, weighing on near-term copper market sentiment and price expectations. | The piece says copper has broken $5/lb as demand accelerates for EV charging stations and AI data centers. It notes copper mining output is growing far more slowly than other base metals. With demand rising and supply constrained, the typical market result is higher copper prices. | The piece claims a new copper “supercycle” is emerging, driven by electrification and clean-energy demand versus constrained supply. It cites shortages by 2025 and immediate disruptions including Panama’s Cobre Panama closure, a Peru mine strike, and Chile output misses/drought. It concludes copper prices should rise. | Nevada Copper reported an update on its Chapter 11 bankruptcy, including interim court approval for US$20m debtor-in-possession financing to fund care-and-maintenance during restructuring. The company plans a sale process and retains advisors. It operates the Pumpkin Hollow copper project, making the situation potentially supply-negative for copper near term. | The piece focuses on Barrick Gold’s Lumwana copper mine in Zambia and its planned “Super Pit” expansion, targeting higher annual copper output and growth from 2024 onward. It highlights Zambia’s role as a major copper producer and the expansion’s costs and timeline, supporting expectations for increased near-term supply. | Nevada Copper reported court orders granting interim approval of debtor-in-possession (DIP) financing, authorizing an initial US$20 million borrowing to fund care-and-maintenance and support for employees during Chapter 11. The company plans a sale process and has appointed new interim executives. The restructuring likely limits copper output.

BHP says South Australia should become a larger “copper province” by growing existing assets at Olympic Dam, Carrapateena and Prominent Hill, consolidating operations after acquiring OZ Minerals, and running extensive exploration. Management highlights current output targets and synergies via centralized smelting, supporting expectations of steady copper supply. | BHP’s representative discusses copper demand drivers from electrification and renewables, and highlights BHP’s South Australia operations, production targets (310–340 kt this FY), and multiple growth projects (Prominent Hill, Carrapateena, Olympic Dam expansion and refinery/smelter studies). The message is investment- and supply-supportive for copper markets. | Copper prices rebounded after hitting an eight-week low, with LME three-month copper up 0.7% and SHFE July copper slightly higher. Traders cited bargain hunting alongside firmer US equities, while weaker Chinese May industrial output growth (5.6% vs forecast 6%) continued to cloud demand prospects. | Copper inched up after falling to an eight-week low as bargain hunters supported bids, with LME three-month copper rising slightly. The rebound was capped by weaker-than-expected China industrial output and weak import premiums, while copper inventories rose. Aluminium and nickel also fell, reflecting subdued overall demand sentiment. | Tudor Gold outlined a 12-month plan to advance its Treaty Creek Goldstorm gold-copper project in British Columbia, including exploration drilling, metallurgical test work focused on producing a copper concentrate, and preparation for a Preliminary Economic Assessment by Q2 2025. The update is development-oriented with no immediate copper supply impact. | Riverside Resources completed the acquisition and title transfer of the Maria Luisa copper property in Sonora, Mexico, consolidating it into its Ariel project and updating drill permitting. The area sits near Mexico’s La Caridad copper mine. With a drill permit in hand, the company plans exploration drilling. | Arc Minerals says it has begun a ~3,000 m maiden exploration drilling program at its Virgo copper project in Botswana, following an encouraging induced polarisation survey that identified potential drill targets. Elementos (ELT) says drilling at its Tasmanian Cleveland project intersected high-grade tin and copper, including 3.8% copper over ~350m depth and a higher-grade interval near 8% copper. Management suggests potential discovery separate from existing resources and plans follow-up targeting copper, tin, silver and gold. | Copper prices edged higher on hopes for additional China economic stimulus, supported by a softer dollar. However, LME copper inventories rose to the highest since 2021 and the cash/3-month spread widened, signaling abundant near-term supply and limiting upside. China’s first major interest-rate cuts in 11 months failed to lift commodities sentiment. Copper futures fell—London down about 1% and Shanghai down 0.86%—to the lowest closes since early April. It already broadened Simbara to cover nickel and tin and now intends to add copper, bauxite, manganese and others. Aluminium bucked the trend. | Lifezone Metals says its Simulus laboratory produced the first-ever refined nickel and copper cathode samples from Kabanga Nickel Project flotation concentrate during semi-continuous pilot hydromet testing. Immediate price impact depends on the scale and ramp-up timeline beyond the initial production. | Ero Copper agreed to earn in for a 60% stake in Vale Base Metals’ Furnas copper project in Brazil’s Carajás region. The deal includes a phased drilling program to deliver scoping, pre-feasibility and DFS studies, supporting longer-run supply prospects and improving development momentum for copper. | Sucden Financial’s head of research says copper’s recent upside was driven mainly by re-engaged speculative long positions, with markets watching COMEX/LME arbitrage. Copper prices fell toward a three-and-a-half month low as concerns about weakening demand in top consumer China weighed on the market. Reuters cited dampened physical demand from price surges and China’s prolonged property crisis and limited stimulus after a political meeting. Other LME/SHFE metals showed minor mixed moves. | Strickland Metals reports preliminary IP survey results at its Obradov Potok target within Serbia’s Rogozna project, suggesting a potential new deposit with associated gold and base-metal indicators. Drilling is ongoing and copper is mentioned among soil geochemistry, but there’s no production or market-moving supply data. Aluminium is not addressed. | Copper prices fell again, hovering near a three-and-a-half month low on demand concerns in China and risk-off sentiment. London copper fell below $9,000/t for the first time since early April, down 1.9% to $8,933.50, amid worries over China’s demand after a political meeting produced no targeted support for growth or property. Physical demand stayed weak with high inventories, pressuring also nickel and aluminium. | Two Las Vegas public works workers were critically injured replacing stolen copper lines. The county says 184 miles of wire were stolen since 2022, prompting work to replace copper and switch some streetlight wiring to overhead aluminum wire. The story implies local replacement demand, but no market-wide supply shocks. | London copper fell below $9,000 for the first time since early April, down 1.4% to $8,981.5/t, driven by investor risk-off sentiment and weaker China demand expectations after no supportive policy measures. Weak physical demand and high inventories were cited, alongside equity losses. Nickel and aluminium also fell. | Kincora Copper announced ANT and gravity surveys at its Nyngan Project with Fleet Space Technologies, aimed at identifying and refining copper porphyry targets ahead of upcoming drilling under an earn-in and joint venture with AngloGold Ashanti. The restart follows improved security and growing China-Taliban ties, potentially supporting copper market sentiment despite short-term constraints. | US copper mine production is forecast to rise 4% in 2024 as output improves at major operations like Robinson, Pinto Valley, and the ramp-up of Pumpkin Hollow. The article cites higher recovery rates, richer ore, and throughput expansions, increasing supply expectations and weighing on copper prices. | The article argues copper remains supported by supply constraints and rising demand expectations tied to AI, electric vehicles, and electricity growth. It cites steep declines in LME and global copper stocks, the Cobre Panama suspension and other producers’ guidance cuts, and notes strong recent copper price gains. | Brixton Metals announced initial 2024 drill assays from its Thorn Project in NW British Columbia, describing copper-dominant porphyry Cu-Au-Ag-Mo mineralization, with multiple higher-grade sub-intervals and extended mineralization in hole THN24-290. The stronger US GDP data and worsening risk sentiment pushed industrial commodities lower, and the article notes copper also declined alongside silver. | An analyst says global copper fundamentals are still weak: China demand is soft, especially tied to the property market, while supply appears “pretty strong,” leaving markets oversupplied. The delay suggests sustained copper supply disruption risk. | The article argues for bullish copper prospects, highlighting tight LME copper inventories and supply constraints tied to mine disruptions such as the suspension of Panama’s Cobre Panama. NorthWest Copper reported results of its 2024 AGM, including re-election of directors, auditor reappointment, and approval of an equity incentive plan. Codelco CEO Ruben Alvarado said the state-run producer is “working very hard” to recover copper output after an 8.4% drop in the first half of 2024, with production at a 25-year low. The release is exploratory and does not mention production, shipments, or new supply disruptions affecting prices. | Chile’s Codelco said it is taking steps to recover copper production after output fell to a 25-year low. Overall, investors may view improved copper-supply prospects as supportive for near-term prices. | BHP and Lundin Mining agreed to buy Filo Corp for $4.53 billion and form a 50:50 JV to advance the Filo del Sol copper project in Argentina and Josemaria in Chile. The city highlights copper theft from the 6th Street bridge, proposes stricter telecom-wire possession rules, and seeks measures to etch/brand public copper wiring. | Merdeka Copper Gold (MDKA) is shifting its mining energy use from coal to renewables such as solar, alongside B35 biodiesel for vehicles and feasibility work for hydropower and wind. The transition is tied to operations including the Wetar copper mine and MDKA’s nickel assets, framed as cost-efficient ESG that may support output continuity. | A newly formed task force in Las Vegas targets copper theft from streetlights and public utilities, citing nearly 971,000 feet of copper wire stolen since early 2022. Officials aim to pass local and state laws to curb sales of stolen copper, potentially reducing theft volumes and streetlight-related losses. | Rio Tinto reported H1 2024 net profit rising to $5.8bn, largely driven by higher copper production and higher copper prices, offsetting lower iron ore prices. The results signal supportive expectations for copper over the coming weeks. | Perenti’s Barminco has finalized terms to continue its five-year underground mining services contract at MMG’s Khoemacau copper mine in Botswana until June 30, 2025, with remaining contract value about $240 million. Copper prices fell at the start of August as concerns grew over weakening Chinese demand, supported by a private survey showing manufacturing contracted and an official PMI hitting a five-month low. Despite some support from a narrower export parity and retreating inventories, higher exports boosted inventories. Powell’s rate-cut hint offered limited offset. | Energy Metals News says copper miners are expanding and restarting operations to address global supply shortages. It highlights exploration and ramp-up at projects including Southern Copper’s Tia Maria restart, production increases at various mines, and new drilling updates. Overall messaging is that added near-term supply can ease tightness while demand grows. | Copper prices fell as China’s factory data came in weaker than expected, reducing near-term demand expectations for the top metal consumer. At the same time, rising LME inventories pointed to excess supply. As a result, three-month LME copper declined 1.9% to around $9,048/ton. | Capstone reported Q2 2024 results including 40,937 tonnes of copper production and improved profitability driven by a higher realized copper price versus the prior year. London copper rose 0.4% but was set for a flat week as investors await US jobs data while China’s demand outlook remains downbeat. Copper inventories stay high. LME aluminium edged down. Nickel prices were supported by tight Indonesian ore supply, but overall the week’s direction appears muted. | Copper prices edged higher as markets increasingly priced potential U.S. interest-rate cuts, supporting the metal via a softer USD backdrop. Physical demand in China improved slightly, with the Yangshan copper premium rising to a three-month peak and SHFE deliverable inventories down, though investors stayed cautious on weak macro data. | Goldrea Resources announced LOIs to acquire 50% interests in additional mineral claims adjoining its Cannonball Project, which hosts multiple porphyry copper-gold and copper-gold prospects. The company says the geology matches Seabridge’s Golden Triangle corridor and highlights recommended drilling. No production volumes, inventories, or near-term supply changes are reported.

London copper edged up 0.2% after prior sell-offs, as some dip-buying emerged, but global demand concerns capped upside. The U.S. jobs report and weaker China activity fueled recession worries while expectations of Fed cuts supported industrial demand. LME aluminium and nickel rose slightly, showing mixed sentiment. | CMOC and other miners in the Democratic Republic of Congo have applied for their copper brands to be registered for delivery against LME contracts. If approved, DRC copper could enter LME warehouses from next year, supporting financing and exchange fee revenue. ESG oversight for DRC sourcing is also discussed. | Article reviews China’s H1 2024 copper cathode rod market, linking moves in copper prices to RRR cuts, smelter processing-fee drops and production cuts, weak real-estate/infrastructure demand, and later a price correction that loosened order pressure. The risk-off move also pushed aluminium and nickel modestly lower alongside copper. | Copper 360 Limited began underground mining at the Rietberg copper mine in South Africa’s Northern Cape. Ore is processed at a newly acquired modular flotation plant, targeting 12,000 tonnes per month in the first month and rising as additional capacity is commissioned. Field sampling found anomalous multi-metal geochemistry and rock chips with up to 0.7% copper, supporting plans to advance projects to drill-ready status. | Copper prices fell to more than four-month lows as concerns over weaker demand in China and the United States triggered a selloff. The Reuters report links the decline to signs of stalled industrial activity in China, weighing on copper used in power and construction, with prices down about 20% from May records. | Copper fell sharply (up to 3.8% on the LME) as global financial contagion triggered a risk-off rush to cash out and turn bearish amid fears of a US recession. The selloff pressured other industrial raw-materials, reflecting weaker growth expectations and tighter financial conditions.

Alderan Resources discusses exploration progress at Utah’s Frisco copper-gold district, including permit approval and a two-stage drilling plan for the New Years prospect, plus follow-up work at the Cactus area. Penalties include fines and possible jail, with a council discussion scheduled for Aug. 7. | LME and Shanghai copper prices extended losses as speculators stayed pessimistic about global growth and reported weak demand in China. The company plans to process ore at Nama modular flotation plants and expects output of about 45,000t per month at 1.6% copper, moving from producing plate to copper concentrate. | Brixton Metals reported assay results from its second Camp Creek drill hole at the Thorn Project, including a 1,126m copper-dominant porphyry interval (CuEq up to ~0.67%) and higher-grade sub-intervals. The findings expand the copper-gold system footprint, but no near-term production changes are stated. | The piece argues the copper market is under intense strain: copper prices are pushing to all-time highs while treatment and refining charges (TC/RCs) have fallen to record lows. The piece notes zinc fell after inventories rose, pointing to oversupply pressure. No direct supply or policy shock for copper is cited beyond macro sentiment.

London copper fell 0.5% to a more than four-and-a-half month low as a firm dollar and looming U.S. recession fears pressured prices, alongside weak China demand concerns. Such divestment can influence copper supply expectations near term, but without specifics on operational continuity or production curtailments, the immediate price impact is likely limited or unclear. | Marimaca Copper announced closing C$30.3 million in private placements led by Assore International Holdings, plus a related acquisition of shares from an affiliate. The funds relate to developing its Antofagasta, Chile oxide heap-leach copper project, with warrants exercisable at C$5.85 over 18 months. | RCMP in Innisfail arrested a repeat offender and seized more than 1,500 pounds of stripped copper wire from a truck and trailer. RCMP in Innisfail arrested a repeat offender suspected of stealing copper wire from industrial/commercial/oilfield sites and seized over 1,500 pounds of stripped copper wire, along with tools and other contraband. While no production or resource numbers are provided, the news is supportive for perceived future copper supply potential. | US Copper Corp retained Global Resource Engineering to complete an updated PEA for its 100% owned Moonlight-Superior copper project in Northeast California. Copper rose Friday after U.S. jobless claims came in better than expected, easing recession-demand fears and supporting rate-cut optimism. It still faces a fifth straight weekly drop, with LME inventories at a high since 2019 limiting upside catalysts. Aluminium and nickel also climbed amid the broader rebound. | North Little Rock’s ball fields were damaged after a copper thief stole underground wiring and electrical components, shutting down parts of the complex. Police estimate the stolen copper value at about $25–$50 but repairs could cost $40,000–$50,000. A $2,000 reward is offered for information leading to an arrest. | Vandals stole copper from telecom infrastructure in Woodend, Victoria, causing a major NBN service outage for about 2,300 customers and disrupting phone service. Police are investigating the theft and ask for footage. The report signals continued copper-targeting, but offers no quantity or market-wide supply impact. | Los Angeles City Council approved an additional $200,000 for its police-led “heavy metal task force” targeting copper wire theft from public infrastructure. The initiative has led to arrests and recovered copper wire. Critics argue outages are often maintenance-related and prefer preventative streetlight solutions like LED or solar power. | Ormonde notes Peak Nickel has resumed a 2024 drill programme at its Rodburn Ni-Cu-Co project in Scotland, targeting further definition and potential expansion of mineralization. The announcement includes Ni, Cu and Co grades used in Ni-equivalent assumptions, but provides no new price-driving results or production impacts. | McEwen Copper says drilling at the Loz Azules mine in Argentina confirmed high-grade copper and that the feasibility study remains on track. Copper prices opened the week softer as investors weighed ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and awaited data for demand signals. Reuters cites economic worries including recession fears, with copper down on both LME and Shanghai. Nickel also slipped, while aluminium was steadier, reflecting cautious industrial demand sentiment. | East Star Resources reported early exploration progress at its Ayagoz and Snowy licences in Kazakhstan, prospective for copper porphyry deposits. LME copper was up about 0.9%, with cancelled warrants increasing and warehouse inventories still elevated. The report also said aluminium and nickel climbed on the same risk-on backdrop. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share in LME-approved warehouses rose to 65% in July (from 50% in June), while Indian-origin fell. Total on-warrant aluminium stocks fell 23% in July. Russian copper and nickel shares declined. The piece also references the LME’s earlier ban on sanctioned Russian output. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share of on-warrant stocks rose to 65% in July while Indian-origin fell to 33%, alongside a 23% decline in total on-warrant aluminium stocks. The May Russian ban remains cited, with similar share changes for copper and nickel inventories. | Kincora Copper announced a funded earn-in arrangement with Woomera Mining for Mongolia’s Bronze Fox copper-gold project, including up to US$4m of exploration spend, drilling starting in September, and potential acquisition of remaining interest or free-carry to a final investment decision. Stainless steel distributors report weak demand, and lower nickel-driven stainless surcharges reduce buyer incentives. U.S. manufacturing PMI stays in contraction, reinforcing soft end-use conditions. Outokumpu sees growth QoQ but still down YoY.

Pan Asia Metals converted an exclusivity agreement into a binding option to buy the Rosario copper project in northern Chile, with fees paid over a three-year option and a final purchase fee. The company plans IP geophysics and further sampling/trenching, citing high-grade historical rock-chip results and proximity to major facilities and El Salvador. | LME copper snapped a three-session rally, falling about 0.6% to near $8,973/ton as investors turned cautious ahead of forthcoming US CPI and China data that could influence Fed rate-cut timing. The Front-End Module will ramp up during Q3 2024. | SMM survey data show China copper cathode-rod operating rates rebounded to 70.52% in July. Copper prices fell, yet the primary–scrap price spread narrowed markedly, drawing orders back from secondary rods to cathode rods and lifting cathode-rod output. August operating rates are expected to rise only slightly to 72.71%. | Jubilee Metals says it has produced first copper concentrate from the newly upgraded front-end section of the Roan concentrator plant in Zambia. The concentrate will be refined at its Sable refinery to make copper cathode, with the Zambian operation now spanning concentrator plus refinery upgrades for over 25,000 tpa. | Morgan Stanley flags nickel may be near its pricing floor: inventories are surging and demand remains challenging, limiting upside and keeping prices range-bound. For copper, spot has retraced and Macquarie cuts short- to medium-term demand and price forecasts, pointing to renewed near-term downside pressure despite M&A activity. | Morgan Stanley says nickel’s “bottom” may be near but elevated nickel inventories and challenging demand constrain sustained rallies, implying range-bound pricing. Macquarie expects copper volatility, cuts 2025–27 forecasts on weaker demand, and lowers 2024 Chinese and North American growth estimates. Overall, neither metal faces a clear immediate breakout. | Reuters reports copper prices fell as LME inventories climbed to near five-year highs and China demand remained sluggish. Traders cited exports of surplus copper into Asian LME warehouses and weaker Chinese macro data. Purchases from some physical consumers and hopes of a BHP Escondida strike limited losses, but overall sentiment stayed bearish. | Rio Tinto says it is prepared to sell stakes in its Western Australia Winu copper project, after earlier plans for alternative partnerships. The move could trigger more consolidation in the Paterson Province, with Newmont also seeking to exit. The news is about project ownership, not immediate output. | The report says BHP workers at its top copper mine went on strike after talks failed. The stoppage raises the risk of reduced copper output and near-term supply tightness expectations, typically weighing on copper sentiment and prices over the coming days as the market assesses duration and restart prospects. | Grid Metals provided a permitting and exploration update for its Manitoba projects. The stoppage threatens near-term copper production and increases supply-risk perceptions. With output disrupted at a major source, traders typically expect tighter physical availability and volatility, influencing copper prices. | Ivanhoe Electric said it completed a final US$10 million option payment to acquire 100% ownership of mineral and surface rights for its Santa Cruz copper project in Arizona. The union represents most frontline operational staff and replacement workers are prohibited by law, raising the risk of reduced output from the world’s largest copper producer and likely tightening near-term supply. | Workers at BHP’s Escondida copper mine in Chile began a strike after contract talks failed, potentially disrupting production at the world’s largest copper source. The stoppage could affect concentrate supply to Chinese smelters, raising short-term supply risk. Markets initially stayed calm, but analysts warned broader labor-dispute contagion. | Workers at BHP’s Escondida copper mine in Chile began a strike after rejecting a wage deal proposal following five days of mediation. Workers at Chile’s Escondida copper mine, producing about 5.4% of global output, went on strike after contract talks with BHP failed. The move likely reduces the immediate risk of lost output and supply disruption, which can pressure short-term copper prices as market participants adjust toward improved near-term availability. | Police arrested a suspect for stealing about 40 copper downspouts worth about $20,000 from a shopping center in San Luis Obispo. With Chile the world’s largest mined copper supplier, the labor actions add to already tight supply conditions, supporting expectations for higher copper prices in coming days. | Nevada Copper Corp. signed an agreement with Southwest Critical Materials LLC (an affiliate of Kinterra Capital) to buy substantially all of its assets for $128 million as a “stalking horse” bidder. White Cliff Minerals announced additional rock-chip assay results from its Great Bear Project in Northern Canada, including multiple high-grade copper grades (up to 42.2% Cu) alongside gold and silver. Research says progress diversifying away from Chinese-backed copper sources is slow. The stoppage could extend beyond 40 days, pressuring near-term copper supply. | London LME three-month copper fell 0.4% as the previous rally was attributed to short-covering rather than new demand. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | Bougainville Copper Ltd and former parent Rio Tinto signed an MoU to remove ageing infrastructure at the long-closed Panguna mine to address safety risks. The report signals ongoing theft activity but gives no copper volume, so market impact is uncertain. | Around 100 copper plaques were stolen from Tweed Heads General Cemetery, prompting council replacement costs estimated at $30,000–$40,000. The results strengthen expectations for large-scale copper mineralization at depth. | Namibia’s Consolidated Copper Corp restarted the Tschudi copper plant after a four-year shutdown, following refurbishment. Copper cathode premiums in Germany and cif Rotterdam stay at multi-month lows, while sellers have ample material, indicating bearish near-term pressure despite some energy-transition demand. | New Destiny Mining reports drilling at its Treasure Mountain project in BC, with Hole 7 intersecting altered porphyry dikes and copper-bearing minerals. Los Angeles announced a crackdown on copper wire thefts, warning residents and offenders of more arrests. As other nearby projects likely won’t reach production until after 2026, the closure raises near-term concerns about copper supply and smelting throughput. Local workforce and investment risks are emphasized.

Rusal reported a 41.6% jump in first-half profit as it increased production of primary aluminium and alumina and boosted shipments to China to record levels. It also notes the LME broad ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel amid U.S./UK sanctions. Improved volumes and rerouting are mildly supportive for prices. | Rusal’s first-half adjusted net profit rose 41.6% as it increased production and shipped record volumes to China. The firm faced demand pressure as Western buyers avoid Russia-made metal after Ukraine. The article notes April LME bans on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel and links improved output to higher prices for aluminium and alumina. | Carnaby Resources and Latitude 66 reported further reverse-circulation drilling results at the Greater Duchess JV near Mt Isa, including multiple high-grade copper intersections and accompanying gold. The findings support prefeasibility work and additional surveys, but they are exploration updates rather than near-term production impacts. | South32 reported a first-time ore reserve life of 16 years for the Sierra Gorda copper mine and confirmed a sulphide stockpile mineral resource update. It also outlines plant debottlenecking and a fourth grinding line expansion targeting higher throughput and lower operating costs, supporting future copper production growth. | Queensland is considering an extra $1.2 billion for the CopperString high-voltage transmission line after costs rose to an estimated $6.2 billion. The project is expected to support mining development and help supply cheaper power, likely boosting near-term expectations for grid metals, especially copper, with possible spillover to aluminium. | London three-month copper rose 0.3% to $9,287/mt as markets priced in U.S. rate cut expectations for September, supporting sentiment. However, a firm U.S. dollar and concerns about China’s demand limited the upside, leading to capped gains after early losses. | Aston Bay says an 8,105 line-km helicopter-borne MobileMT EM survey is underway and it staked additional claims at its Epworth copper-silver project in Nunavut, after sampling and mapping. Results are expected to guide targeting for anticipated drilling in 2025, not near-term production. | Argentina’s minister says the pro-business mining incentive regime (RIGI) should attract major foreign investment and help new lithium and copper projects start later this year. Copper is framed as crucial for EVs and AI, with large capex needs for projects, implying improved near-term supply/export expectations. | Cordoba Minerals announced Colombia’s National Mining Agency approved the PTO for the Alacran copper-gold-silver project. The permit is required to license the mine for construction, moving the project closer to an anticipated Q2 2025 start, subject to financing, which may modestly support copper supply expectations. | Grid Battery Metals acquired 100% interest in 17 mineral claims (27,525 ha) in northern-central British Columbia, adding a new copper property. The release describes regional copper-gold geology and adjacent projects but includes no drilling results, production changes, or supply-chain disruptions, suggesting minimal short-term impact on copper prices. | Cordoba Minerals said Colombia’s National Mining Agency approved the Mining Technical Work Plan (PTO) for its Alacran copper-gold-silver project. The PTO is a key permit toward mine construction, expected to start in Q2 2025 if financing is secured. The update is a milestone, not near-term output. | Quetzal Copper provided an update on drilling and geophysical work at its Cristinas Project in Chihuahua, Mexico, completing a downhole InfiniTEM survey and planning to recommence drilling in September 2024. It highlights the presence of historic shallow copper oxide mineralization and aims to model results for next drill targets. | Hudbay said Arizona’s ADEQ has issued the aquifer protection permit for its Copper World project. The permit completes one of three key state prerequisites, de-risking advancement toward feasibility work and potential sanctioning. Copper World is expected to produce about 85,000 tonnes of copper annually.

Queensland Treasurer Cameron Dick says an extra $1.2 billion for the CopperString 840-kilometre transmission project would be provided only if the government is re-elected. The project’s costs have reportedly risen more than 20%. The mayor argues conditional funding could destabilize confidence and affect financing and timelines. | Arizona approved a key aquifer protection permit for Hudbay’s Copper World open-pit copper mine, with additional monitoring wells and conditions to protect groundwater. The approval enables construction to proceed subject to one remaining air-quality permit. The news is project-progress positive but lacks near-term production timing, limiting immediate copper-price pressure. | Hudbay Minerals said Arizona ADEQ issued the Aquifer Protection Permit for its Copper World project, a key state requirement for sanctioned development. Copper World is expected to produce about 85,000 tonnes of copper annually, but production is targeted for later (around 2026). The permit advances feasibility and project planning. | BHP said South Australia has begun the application and assessment process for expanding the Olympic Dam copper smelter and refinery. The move supports plans to grow copper cathode output in coming years, with a final investment decision in 2027. The news could influence future supply expectations but timing limits immediate price pressure. | Camino Minerals and Nittetsu extend by one week their exclusive rights to negotiate the acquisition of the Puquios Project, a construction-ready copper mine in Chile. The deal remains subject to approvals and cannot be assured. The update may modestly boost copper sentiment by improving prospects for future supply. | Camino Minerals and Nittetsu extended their one-week exclusive rights to finalize negotiations for the acquisition of Chile’s Puquios Project, a construction-ready copper mine. The news is a progress/contracting update with no stated change to near-term production, but it may modestly influence copper supply expectations. | First Quantum is seeking damages from Panama after its Cobre Panama copper mine was shut following a Supreme Court ruling of unconstitutionality. A stockpile of about 120,000 tonnes of copper ore remains on site pending arbitration, with claims tied to letting the material leave or compensating at market value. | BHP says it is advancing plans to expand the Olympic Dam copper smelter and refinery in South Australia, with state approval pending. The project targets higher refined copper cathode output, potentially rising from 322,000 tons to 500,000 tons by the early 2030s, amid broader copper demand expectations. | Copper prices rose on optimism that China, the top metals consumer, may receive further government support for its economy. Investors also increasingly believe prices bottomed earlier in the month. The move put copper on course for its first monthly gain since May, when it hit a record above $11,100/t.

Lotte Energy Materials signed an agreement to supply copper foil for StarPlus Energy’s first EV battery plant in Indiana. The Stellantis–Samsung SDI JV is building two plants with total investment of $6.4bn, aiming for 33 GWh capacity per year from the first plant in 1Q2025. Copper foil demand outlook improves.

Golden Deeps shares surged after intersecting an 80m zone of semi-massive copper–zinc sulphides at its Havilah NSW project, with field results showing up to 18.5% copper and 34.8% zinc. Management believes it may indicate a large porphyry-sulphide system, pending lab assays. | The article headline says Goldman cut its copper price forecast by $5,000 after closing a bullish position. That signals weaker near-term expectations for copper demand and/or pricing, which can weigh on copper futures and related market sentiment over the next days to weeks. | ICBC denies liability in a dispute with ING over alleged trade-finance losses tied to documents against payment copper shipments routed through Maike Metals. The Hong Kong court rejected ICBC’s bid to move the case to mainland China, citing deviation from URC collection instructions. Maike later collapsed amid a liquidity crisis. | Orestone Mining optioned the Las Burras-Incahuasi copper-gold-molybdenum porphyry property in Argentina, citing prior drill intersections up to 0.42% copper over 112 metres and planning an AirMT survey and further drilling. The update is about exploration advancement, with no near-term production affecting copper prices. | The piece reviews copper cathode inventories: SHFE stocks rose post–Chinese New Year, while LME/COMEX were historically low before turning higher on continued exports. Despite exchange visible-stock buildup, SMM shows China social and smelter inventories declining. It concludes global inventory growth may slow as China continues destocking into peak season. | ERG announced a $150m pre-export finance facility backed by a copper cathode supply agreement from its Metalkol operation. Glencore and Bank of China are counterparties, supporting working capital and ongoing exports from the DRC, where copper production is emphasized for global customers. | New Destiny Mining reported prospecting at its Treasure Mountain project in BC, identifying a Spokane quartz vein with up to 6.6% copper (plus 100 g/t silver) from handheld XRF spot measurements. Samples are being sent for lab assays and ICP, with further prospecting and permitting planned. | Producers of metals slid after Goldman Sachs warned that China’s economy remains listless, with ongoing copper demand likely to weaken more than supply concerns.
