ADEM and Ecovery entered a consent order over alleged air-permit violations at an Alabama secondary aluminum recycling facility. The plant processes aluminum-copper radiators and had visible particulate emissions linked to south dust collector maintenance. The firm replaced filters and seals; later stack tests showed compliance. A $15,000 civil penalty was assessed. | Electroninks launched copper MOD ink for advanced semiconductor packaging, targeting fine-line metallization/RDL seed layer printing. The company says ink-based printing can displace some electroless copper plating and PVD tie layers while improving throughput and reducing water/energy use. The report highlights copper as a standard in electronics but gives no production volumes. | Ivanhoe Mines said its Kamoa-Kakula copper complex in the DRC produced a monthly record 40,347 tonnes of copper in concentrate in August and a daily record 2,096 tonnes on Aug. 31. Hot commissioning of Phase 3 fine-grinding mills is expected to finish in coming days, lifting recoveries toward 86% and supporting 2024 guidance. | Scotia’s Orest Wowkodaw says copper miners still look expensive, estimating miners imply an average copper price of about $5.06/lb versus spot near $4.00/lb (a ~27% premium). The premium is attributed to strong investor appetite and M&A speculation, alongside lower by-product prices. Macro NFP risk is noted but not copper-specific. | Mink Ventures reported positive results from a summer sampling program at its Warren nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario. Reference samples confirmed mineralized zones with meaningful nickel grades and significant copper assays, supporting drill targeting for a planned diamond drilling phase after freeze-up. | Mink Ventures reported positive grab-sample assays from the summer sampling program at its Warren nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario, highlighting multiple zones with copper and nickel grades. The company plans winter access and diamond drilling after freeze-up to test refined IP/EM targets. Near-term impact on metal prices appears limited. | Eurasian Resources Group secured a US$150mn pre-export finance facility with Glencore and Bank of China, backed by copper cathode supply from its Metalkol plant in Kolwezi, DRC. The deal supports working capital and ongoing copper deliveries, reinforcing near-term supply expectations as DRC copper output underpins global demand. | Osisko’s asset update includes a major copper focus via its interests in the CSA Mine, where Metals Acquisition reported record quarterly copper production of 10,864 tonnes and record monthly output in June, alongside stronger grades and new drilling intercepts. This supports expectations of steady copper supply. | Iran’s newly appointed industries minister said the government will accelerate the Janja copper-and-gold project in Sistan to boost regional jobs and investment. Authorities plan extra funding for faster development by contractors. The mine is sizable, but first-year copper cathode/concentrate output is scheduled for 2028.

Las Vegas City Council approved an ordinance to curb copper wire theft, making it illegal to buy scrap metal without transport by motor vehicle and limiting purchases of burned scrap insulation unless sellers provide proof of legal origin. Local businesses like Good Pie and Cork and Thorn were victims, but no market-wide copper impact is quantified. | Las Vegas passed an ordinance to curb copper theft, raising misdemeanor fines and restricting cash transactions for recycling. City leaders say stolen copper from infrastructure delays repairs and costs millions. Businesses report tens of thousands in losses when thieves strip wiring, while recycling yards help implement the regional crackdown. | Police in KwaZulu-Natal recovered about R20 million of stolen copper cables from a scrapyard in Queensburgh after an investigation and operation involving the Hawks. The eThekwini municipality said much of the copper belonged to it. A scrapyard manager was arrested, following prior cable theft recoveries in Durban. | Investors are reducing copper forecasts and unwinding bullish positions as concerns grow over short-term oversupply and weakening Chinese demand. Goldman Sachs exited a long position and cut its 2025 copper target by about one-third to an average of $10,100/tonne, signaling softer price expectations.

Galileo Resources updated on the Luansobe copper project in Zambia. A mining schedule and pit optimisations were completed, with scenarios using 0.25% and 0.5% Cu cut-offs yielding ~70,000t and ~40,000t of projected open-pit copper, plus prospects for additional shallow and deeper underground resources. Permitting and funding remain key constraints. | Galileo Resources updated shareholders on development progress for the Luansobe copper project in Zambia, including granted small-scale mining licence and mine-design work. The company discussed open-pit optimization scenarios yielding about 40,000–70,000 tonnes of projected copper, but production remains dependent on permits and funding. | Armenia’s copper concentrate production rose 14.5% in January–July 2024 to 172,937.4 tons, per the National Statistical Committee. The report also notes zinc output up, while molybdenum concentrate and ferromolybdenum production fell. The copper increase suggests greater supply that could weigh on copper prices. | ARM says weaker platinum group metals and coal prices cut its annual profit, prompting a pivot toward chrome and copper. It approved a chrome recovery plant at Bokoni and acquired a 15% stake in Surge Copper. South African miners also eye copper assets, but timing to production is long-dated. | Camino Minerals and Nittetsu extended their exclusive negotiating rights by one week to finalize documentation for the proposed acquisition of Chile’s Puquios project, a construction-ready copper mine. The deal remains conditional on approvals and deal completion by Sept. 14, leaving some uncertainty on timing. | Canada Nickel said Export Development Canada is considering a loan of up to $500 million for the Crawford project in Ontario and that the company is seeking remaining funding and permits for construction. It also plans a nickel-processing plant in Ontario to process about 80,000 tonnes annually, supporting nickel supply expectations. | The piece argues global copper demand is rising from electrification and energy transition while supply faces constraints: declining ore grades, labor disruptions, fewer high-quality projects, and a large projected deficit by 2030. It details BHP’s expansion in South Australia (including refining) but says uncertainty remains about fully closing the gap. | China filed a WTO complaint against Canada over Canadian tariffs on Chinese EVs plus 25% tariffs on steel and aluminium. Canada’s measures take effect next month and are aimed at unfair pricing and environmental/labour standards. China signals retaliation risk, adding near-term uncertainty for industrial demand. | KwaZulu-Natal officials praised law enforcement for arresting a suspect linked to theft of copper worth over R20 million. The recovered copper was found in a scrapyard near Durban. Officials said stolen metals are sold to scrapyards, harming municipalities’ infrastructure, and urged community reporting to combat copper theft. | China has filed a WTO complaint against Canada over Canada’s new tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles and 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum, with Canada’s measures set to start next month. Analysts warn retaliation could raise costs and pressure markets, adding near-term uncertainty for aluminum supply and prices. | Harmony Gold plans to begin copper production from its Eva mine in Queensland by 2028, after a two-year build and a final investment decision by end of the year, contingent on a Queensland grant. The project is expected to produce up to 60,000 tpa, but near-term copper supply impact is limited.

Terrain Minerals says a recent airborne VTEM survey identified five bedrock sulphide conductors, including two within its “eye” target in a mafic/ultramafic intrusive setting, a geological style associated with nickel/copper deposits. It frames this as underexplored and potentially economic, though no drilling results are yet provided. | Ducab Metals Business (DMB) plans to double annual aluminium capacity from 55,000tpa to 110,000tpa at KEZAD and expand bare copper output. The company frames the move as supporting growing global demand and UAE industrial strategy, including green aluminium initiatives. Goldman cut its 2025 copper forecast by nearly a third, citing weaker-than-expected China demand and a less constructive commodities view. Technical resistance supports further declines, weighing on copper-linked AUD. | SMM survey shows China’s copper cathode rod operating rate rose to 74.62% in August, above expectations, and is forecast to rise to 75.86% in September. The report links stronger downstream orders to recent copper price declines below 72,000 yuan/mt and suggests improved production and sales into early September. | Falcon Gold mobilizes drill crews to begin an inaugural drilling campaign at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland after prior access delays. The release cites high-grade prior copper drilling, outlines testing of geophysical targets, and notes ongoing permitting for additional drilling. | Fastmarkets’ podcast transcript with Freeport-McMoRan CEO Kathleen Quirk highlights copper’s demand growth from decarbonization, renewables and EVs, with China still driving over half of global consumption. It also notes dwindling new viable projects and inventory buildup, suggesting a supportive near-term market outlook. | Falcon Gold says drill crews and equipment are being mobilized to its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland after spring access delays from high water. The firm completed 3 km of road. The property hosts reported copper-rich zones, with prior results from neighboring drilling indicating strong copper grades. | Nevada Copper updated its Chapter 11 sale process and designated Southwest Critical Minerals LLC, an affiliate of Kinterra Capital, as the successful bidder. The company expects court approvals and a potential October closing. It owns the Pumpkin Hollow copper project, but the release doesn’t specify near-term production impacts. | Residents in Mammoth, Arizona debate whether exploration for a proposed copper mine on Copper Creek could threaten landscape and groundwater.
